It’s different than just wearing sweatpants and these actually look sporty and trendy.

Nice inexpensive pants . Can dress up or go causal . Comfortable Over all I like the pants . The material Is soft and has a nice stretch to them . I’m 5’2 and surprisingly that weren’t super long on me which I was nervous about . I wore a wedge heel with them and they were fine . I originally ordered an xl because I’m not super flat in the abs but they were too big so I reorder in a large . I probably Could have worn a medium for a tighter fit but I didn’t want them super tight . The only things I didn’t like was they are see through so if You wear a printed shirt that you wanna tuck in you can see the print of the shirt through the pants . So be mindful of that and what ever undergarments you wear underneath . And also I would have liked the buttons to be sewn a little tighter since they add to the style If the pants . ( they are decorative only ) the pants zip in the bank . Over all I would recommend .

Remember home economics? So, this product does what it does. You’re looking for a pair of black slacks minus the frump. This is it. I’ll say, if you ever were an “extra credits” home economics student, this would be your project. Super simple design. Standard material. No frills. I ended up changing the buttons because the default buttons really lowered the value of the pants. They also dangled. I went to Michael’s and got a nice set of buttons and simply replaced the originals with a chrome looking button. Otherwise, the slacks will hug your curves and are very comfortable. I’m 5’5” and weigh 208. I bought an xl confidently.

Serviceable but Don't Love It I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

So close to perfect.. I have been searching the this style of pants for quite a while now and these are *almost* perfect. The material is wrinkle resistant and super lightweight - perfect for summer. Not see through! The back of the waistband is elastic so you can wear it with a tight shirt or chunky sweater without issue. I wish I had sized down because they're not as snug as I'd have liked. Nice big pockets. Seam work is consistent but I did find quite a few loose threads that I had to snip, not ideal but I expected as much. The biggest drawback for me is the length. The hem is just small enough so that letting it down wouldn't quite fix it. I would 100% buy more if they had a long/tall option. I'll still be wearing these often because they are so comfy and I love the style. *PLEASE* ADD TALL SIZE OPTIONS!! I ordered a size 4. My measurements are: 5'9" 130lbs 31" inseam 26"-27" waist 36" hip

Cute and comfy corduroys! This pair of corduroy pants is very comfortable. They're lined on the inside and feel breathable. I really like that they come with front pockets and belt loops. The only thing to note is that they're listed as 100% cotton, but there is no fabric content anywhere on the tag of them, so whether they are or not is questionable. Also, the zipper feels a bit cheap. For reference, I'm 5'3", 100lbs, size 2, wearing small, and they are a relaxed, loose fit and a little long on me uncuffed.

Nice for summer. As someone who practically lives in jeans, I’m always on the lookout for something lightweight to wear when it’s warm. These cropped, gauzy pants looked like a great option, so I selected the neutral khaki pair. Here’s a list of what I liked and what I didn’t.What I liked:• The pants are a cotton/linen blend which makes a soft, lightweight fabric that keeps you cool.• They are washable on a gentle cycle in cold water. I hung them to dry, and they looked ready to wear afterwards.• The color didn’t fade.• The length was great and ended right above my ankles.What I didn’t like:• According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart and the Amazon recommendation, I selected a size large (my usual size). On me (5’6”, 185 pounds, size 12), these pants seemed to run a little small. The waist was comfortable, but they fit slightly small through the hips. As a result, I didn’t get the casual, flowy look that I was wanting.• As much as I love pockets, I don’t like the ones on these pants. They are positioned on the outermost sides of your hips. These pants probably would have looked and fit better without them.I tried these pants with both a cropped t-shirt and a long t-shirt. Because of the fit through the hips, I didn’t care for the look on me when I tried them with the cropped shirt. I preferred the look of the longer shirt over the pants. It created more of the casual, flowy look that I was hoping for. I wore these pants for a day of errands and was delighted with how comfortable they were. I was secretly hoping that the fabric would stretch out through the hip area, but it didn’t (maybe a tiny bit).Overall, these are a nice, lightweight summer pant. I might consider sizing up if you want them looser through the hip. Additionally, I’d probably remove the drawstring, so you don’t see the bulky bow underneath a longer shirt. I’m giving these pants four stars because of the minor sizing issue and the unattractive pockets.

Great color & comfort ~ Size small = fairly big + high rise Just got a compliment on these from someone in my house & she is glad to take these off my hands as they're pretty massive on me ~ Hip = 45" / Front rise 13.5" / Back Rise 18.5" I am 5'4 and pulling up to my belly button I still have 2' left hanging down in the crotch (and also must use the drawstring even to get them to stay as the elastic is also made for someone a bit larger). So it's not a fix to make them look tighter/more fitted just pulling them up unless you are taller with a wider hip. I range from xs to 4/6 all depends but these to me feel like a medium or like one of those very generous vanity sizing smalls. Also just realized the knees stretch so I get the kangaroo look like I'm bending my knees when I'm standing straight. The front panels of fabric down the legs are for some reason narrower than the back, so essentially you can see both inseams & outward seams from the front & not sure what that's doing to the silouette & wear. The wide hip takes away from the wide leg because it's almost seems like it's tapering, even if it's the same width.. there's just a whole lot more upper fabric + rise for me than I'm personally used to in a pant. Nice saturated teal just as in the ad, very drapey and linen-y to some extent (like linen washed a lot and blended with something else) There is no materials tag in the pant, only a brand tag with size & care tag on reverse of it. If you got a lot of hip, are preg or looking for a not-too restrictive short pant with a wide leg & high rise, give em a try! :)

Pretty nice linen wide leg pants! These lineny pants are made of a heavier material with a linen feel to it. They could possibly be hotter than I would like for summer but are not itchy, like most linen pants are for me. I’m 5’4”, so these are full length pants for me instead of capris, but I expected this. I would estimate the inseam length to be 28-29” in the XXL size. Their measurements of the length of the whole pant isn’t all that useful. They did fit perfectly in my normal size and are actually wide leg. These pants will be nice for spring, cool summer nights, and fall. The navy color is a bit darker than their picture and more of a truer navy color which I like better than the brighter blue in their pictures. They did arrive a bit wrinkled and folded into a small box for some odd reason. I’m hoping they will hang out, because this crinkled material won’t iron well. I can’t say yet about how they wash up but will update review if they shrink too much once it gets warm enough to wear them!

Perfect high waist flattering fit, but petite still very long Wow. Love this for the high waist fit that is flattering and stays up- could only find this with lululemon or more expensive brands previously since my body shape is more straight and less curvy in the hips. Great fit, definitely true to size or size down. However, I’m almost 5’6 but have shorter legs and the petite inseam is still too long. Otherwise, amazing find!

Love them! These are my new favorite pants. I wear them for walking and just for running around or lounging around. I think they will be comfortable for travel. The fit is perfect for me. I am 5'3", 160 lbs. The large petite fits fine, comfortably snug without being tight. I initially bought XL from the Skechers website (OG version in both XL & L petite, although I feel really no difference between those and this Joy version). The XL also fits fine without being baggy. I wear both, but I prefer the fit of the L. The top hits just above my belly button. The length is good on me, it will cover the tongue of my sneakers, does not touch the ground. Again, perfect. They feel a little stiff out of the bag but they soften up after the first wash, with no discernible shrinkage. Great quality! I have now bought several pairs. I can only speak for how the black color fits (88% nylon/12% spandex); I know some colors/blends sometimes fit differently. Get these, you will not be disappointed.

Nice high waist! I love these pants! They are so much of everything that this mom of 2 needs! They are high waisted and cover that “mommy” pouch, they have multiple flat pockets for everything I need to carry but dont always want to on a walk (chapstick, money, keys). They are a cute boot cut and not overly flared - I am 5’3 and the come just above my ankle - at first I was not sure how this would translate but they have that modern look of the cropped boot cut jeans...and I am liking it right now. The material has a nice stretch but also holds you in and the waist band is nice and thick!

Super comfortable and look like real pants! ***edited a year postpartum and these are some of my favorite work pants. I'm around 170lbs** The product page is unavailable at time of review, so I can't attest to the monetary value of this product, but I LOVE these pants!!!!! They actually look and feel like a pair of maternity jeggings I have. They have real pockets on the back, so they look like legit pants and not yoga pants (I'm going to try to get away with wearing these suckers to work). The material is a little on the thicker side (not running legging material, thicker than that), but not too sweltering or uncomfortable. I like the boot cut style as well. I feel like this style is more flattering and slimming if you have wider hips (like I do) than the typical "skinny" style that is so popular. These remind me a lot of an active/lounge pair of pants I have from old navy. I like the high rise wait to help tuck in the mom tummy. These are going to be perfect for postpartum wear! The length is great on my as well, not high waters, but not dragging the ground when I have tennis shoes on. For size reference (ignore the preggo belly in the pics...I pulled the waist up so you can see where it's supposed to sit, but not comfy on the bump...will be GREAT once baby comes out for some tummy support): I'm 5'5", currently 185 pounds at 36 weeks pregnant (expect to be around 170 for awhile after baby comes out). I typically wear a Large in work out leggings and these are an XL. I would say these fit true to size. They fit really well on my pregnancy body, which is about 40 pounds heavier than my non preggo body. These will fit great for the first few months postpartum, but then I'd most likely need to size down to a Large. Would absolutely recommend!

Super comfortable and look like real pants! ***edited a year postpartum and these are some of my favorite work pants. I'm around 170lbs** The product page is unavailable at time of review, so I can't attest to the monetary value of this product, but I LOVE these pants!!!!! They actually look and feel like a pair of maternity jeggings I have. They have real pockets on the back, so they look like legit pants and not yoga pants (I'm going to try to get away with wearing these suckers to work). The material is a little on the thicker side (not running legging material, thicker than that), but not too sweltering or uncomfortable. I like the boot cut style as well. I feel like this style is more flattering and slimming if you have wider hips (like I do) than the typical "skinny" style that is so popular. These remind me a lot of an active/lounge pair of pants I have from old navy. I like the high rise wait to help tuck in the mom tummy. These are going to be perfect for postpartum wear! The length is great on my as well, not high waters, but not dragging the ground when I have tennis shoes on. For size reference (ignore the preggo belly in the pics...I pulled the waist up so you can see where it's supposed to sit, but not comfy on the bump...will be GREAT once baby comes out for some tummy support): I'm 5'5", currently 185 pounds at 36 weeks pregnant (expect to be around 170 for awhile after baby comes out). I typically wear a Large in work out leggings and these are an XL. I would say these fit true to size. They fit really well on my pregnancy body, which is about 40 pounds heavier than my non preggo body. These will fit great for the first few months postpartum, but then I'd most likely need to size down to a Large. Would absolutely recommend!

Really thick, good for cold weather I found these walking pants to be a great fit. The only issue might be the high waistband being hard to put on over your hips if you're wide. The waistband goes up over my bellybutton. The length is good for me, perfect when I have sneakers on, I am about 5'6". The are 2 outer back pockets, which are fun to put your hands in. There is a pocket on the left hand side of the inside waist that's about big enough for a small phone... mine wouldn't fit in there. The fabric is very thick, and I think these pants will be warm enough to use outside during the winter for walking. The black is very slimming along with the slight ankle flair. They wash up well and I air dry them.

GREAT LENGTH FOR TALLER WOMEN BUT YOUR GOING TO NEED A LINT ROLLER! These GoFlex Skechers 3 Pocket High Waisted Pants are Super Comfortable and Great For "Lazy" days or Slounging.. After wearing them all day they did not "slide" down or bunch up around the knees, so they have a good solid stretch. I'm 5'10 180 pounds and the Large fit me just right, even in the length. The Measurements For The Large: 17" Across the waist, 11" Rise and 32" Inseam. The description said 3 pocket but I had a little trouble finding the 3rd pocket, Spoiler Alert: It's sewn inside the waistband near your bellybutton. They're made from 88% Nylon 12% Spandex, moisture wicking material that feels good and moves with you but it seems to attract every dog hair in my house, including the ones on my dogs. If you are an owner of an animal with hair, be prepared to have a lint roller.

So Comfy! These are my new favorite pants. I wear them for walking, for running errands or just lounging around. I’m sure they will be comfortable for travel. The fit is perfect for me. I am 5'3", 160 lbs. The Large Petite fits fine, comfortably snug without being tight. I believe these are what Skechers calls their “OG” pant on their website. I feel really no difference between this OG and the Joy version (of which I also purchased one). The XL petite also fits fine without being baggy. I wear both, but I prefer the fit of the L. The top hits just above my belly button. The length is good on me, it will cover the tongue of my sneakers, does not touch the ground. They feel a little stiff out of the bag but they soften up after the first wash, with no discernible shrinkage, and they hold their shape. Great quality! The side pockets are a huge plus, deep enough to fit my large phone with no fear of it popping out as I move. I have now bought several pairs of these OG pants. I can only speak for how the black color fits (88% nylon/12% spandex); I know some colors/blends sometimes fit differently. Get these, you will not be disappointed.

I can't stand the saggy crotch I bought these in an XL last year when I had gained the "Covid 20". At 5'6" and 180 that seemed right. They were very flattering, but the crotch wouldn't stay up. I was constantly tugging to pull them up. I thought maybe the legs were tighter than they should have been and that was pulling them down. I put them away for another day. Now, I'm 163, hips 42. Same problem. Still flattering, but awfully uncomfortable when the crotch sags. The whole pant slides down. Now I'm wondering if the pant is too big or the rise is too short. Either way, I have two of these and will put them in the Goodwill box. Don't know if the pics will show the difference between when first pulled up and after sag happens.

It has 4 pockets! Very versatile, thick, stretchy and comfy! I like these pants. They are a thick Lycra blend and stretchy, great for working out. I like to buy my pants a size larger so I can wear them as lounge wear around the house. These pants are XL and I’m 5’5” and 165. I like pants that don’t grab or grip making me look extra sausage-ey, so I bought this in XL even though a L would have been more comfortable for actually working out. I was surprised to find that these have 4 pockets, 1 on each side and 2 in the back. These make a great pair of stay home everyday pant, soft and comfy and very versatile.

Disappointing I've been looking for a pair of bright white pull-on, straight leg pants for a while now. I thought I'd found them with these Slim-Sation pants. Pros: nice bright white color, perfect length, perfect fit in the legs. However... the back of the waistband gaps terribly!!! Please see photos taken from back and side. (Ignore the butt, please.) I have to continuously keep pulling them up in the back. I am 5'6", 120 lbs. and purchased size 4. Everything fits well, except for the way the back portion of the waistband is designed. There is a separate section below the waistband that just gaps out very weirdly. Unfortunately, I've worn these pants, so cannot return them. But, I am so disappointed that after paying $67 (with tax) for a pair of pants, this is the sad look that I'm stuck with. Oh well... keep looking. I plan to send this same message to the seller and hopefully I'll get an explanation as to the waistband design. Hope this review helps others.

Runs large Im 5'1" and 130 pounds and these can easily be rolled 4 inches. For the price, I'm having them tailored so I can wear them and have them look nice. The pic is with them rolled in. They run loose in the tummy and more fitted thru the legs but I'm thicker legged so maybe it is me. The material is good, the pants are flattering, and I would recommend for a tall curvy body.

Perfect Hiking Pant! First of all - I have a difficult time finding pants that truly fit me. I am 5'2", with a small waist and a bit full in the hips. Usually, I wear size 6, sometimes size 8. These come in XS - 2XL. I took my measurements and according to the size chart, I should fit into a size S. So, I took a chance and ordered them, and I'm glad I did - they fit me perfectly! I love these pants because: the price is right, the overall size is perfect, (even the length) they are well made with material that is water-repellent, is both lightweight and sturdy enough for hiking, the elastic waistband is thick but comfortable and easily adjustable with drawstrings, there are multiple pockets and the pants are convertible to shorts with good quality zippers, and the length of the short is at the perfect height above the knee. I ordered black because I can pair them with any color and I'm glad I ordered the brown because it is a beautiful, deep mocha brown - very pretty! I will be hiking in the Alps next July and I think 2 pair will be enough for 8 days, but I'm already looking for an excuse to buy a third pair! I highly recommend them!!

Plenty of pockets I'm a 5'1" curvy girl who doesn't like tight pants, therefore I'm very picky about hiking pants since most have no stretch and are way too long. I was pleasantly surprised that these pants are a relaxed fit that have room without being too large. They have a hint of stretch, but are not what I'd call actually stretchy. As expected, they have a zip off leg to turn them into shorts. The positive, it's far enough down the leg that you can comfortably hike or climb without worrying about modesty, but it's not so long as to be clear down at your knee. These pants aren't have nicely appointed pockets, with the side pockets being large enough for plus sized cell phones. All pockets have either a strong Velcro closure or a zip closure so items will stay secure. An added bonus for a short girl that I seldom find: a zipper and elasticized cuff at the ankle (yay me!) The zipper makes it easier to put a hiking boot on while the elastic keeps these from being so long that I'm walking on them.

Comfortable and Practical Hiking Pants I ordered these hiking pants, and they've quickly become my go-to for outdoor activities. The material is lightweight and quick-drying, which makes them perfect for camping, hiking, or even just casual wear. The convertible zip-off feature is a nice touch, allowing you to easily switch from pants to shorts when the weather changes. The pants are super comfortable, though I probably could have gone with a size medium instead of large since they’re a bit baggy in the butt area for my height and weight (I'm 5'2" and 148 lbs). Even with the slightly loose fit, they’re still great for moving around and staying active. Overall, these pants are a solid choice for anyone who needs versatile and comfortable gear for the outdoors.

On the fence with this pair of pants I was looking for some inexpensive lightweight joggers to wear hiking or while cleaning the house or yard as I've always been afraid to wear my ridiculously expensive Athleta joggers doing either. I received this pair yesterday and here are my thoughts: Pro: 1. Color options are virtuously infinite and price point is excellent. I ordered the khaki, but was going to consider other colors if the fit was right. 2. Fabric is lightweight, but opaque, doesn't show panty lines, very stretchy, nice drape and soft to the touch, just like the Athleta joggers. 3. Lots of pockets: slant (not bulky) pockets in the front, one zippered pocket over the lumbar spine (for keys or a very tiny pet maybe? too shallow for a phone). 4. Length works for me. I am 5'2", the bottom sags some which is totally fine with me, even gives it a bit of a cool vibe. This pant should easily work for average height women, lengthwise. (Only one) Con: 1. The waist is a tad tight. I ordered a medium which is what Amazon recommended and, while the fabric is overall very stretchy, the very top of the pants where it falls on the waist has a double seam on the inside which gives that area much less give. Picture of waist unstretched is attached. I don't think I'd exchange it for a large, that would probably be too big. In all, I've decided to keep these and see if the waist thing becomes a non-issue later. For reference, my natural waist is 30 inches and I wear a size 6 in Athleta joggers.

True Petite Joggers! The Medium black 25.5 inseam joggers worked great on my 5’4 152lb frame - and will likely look even a bit better if I ever see sub 150 lbs again ;). They came out the package wrinkled as all get-out, but a quick stream should take care of that. They are form fitting, but not too much and pair well with a merino wool t-shirt and cute sneakers for a semi-elevated plane travel outfit. The are a substantial, light-weight slicker material. The black are not transparent, not too thin where you can see every bump and lump (think of that summer tech stretch material that is soooo lightweight) but definitely not an outdoor-activity winter weight. Hopefully they will be just right for cold planes! The patch pockets are a pleasant surprise. With my hips and derrière, on seam tradition pockets tend to show through the material. These are more meant to be noticed since they sit on top of the main pant leg and I am liking that so far. I’ll update if they don’t hold up after my first international business trip but so far, they’re on the list for summer vacay travel.

Soft, but…….. I ordered two pairs of these buttery soft joggers. They are great because they come in different lengths. I opted for the petites which was perfect for my 5’3” body. However, the attention to detail just isn't there. Both pairs of pants are full of bunches of threads both inside and outside of the pants. The one pair I received had very clearly been opened and returned. They were wrinkled beyond words. If you don’t mind purchasing something and then trimming the mounds of threads left behind by the manufacturer, buy these pants otherwise, move on.

Length is good without being to long. I'm 5’5 and they fit nicely in length. I got the black color and it’s a really rich looking pant.

The BEST sweatpants! I live in New England and after a serious search for sweatpants that are open on the bottom... these the the bomb! They are thick enough to not let the breeze through. They are SUPER soft on the inside. The pockets are deep enough to hold an iPhone 13 Plus and it won't fall out. AND THEY DON'T SHRINK IF YOU FOLLOW THE WASHING INSTRUCTIONS! These are to be hung dry. I normally wear a large and have been losing weight so my tops are some mediums but pants are a bit tricky. I would definitely size up if your looking for a bit of a relaxed look as shown in the photos. But they are long enough, are SO COMFORTABLE, and very well made! I will be purchasing more when they are back in stock! Thank you Southpole! You are my savior!

Super-Soft Well-Designed Womens Leggings In my personal (and unsponsored) search for the perfect yoga legging life pant, I purchased both sized XS and SMALL Sp3lops women's legging/pants, in both the waist-pocket style and the side-leg pocket style in Black, Teal, Navy and Maroon.COLOR: First of all, the colors are attractive but off from names IMO. Maroon is a nice berry red, navy has strong purple undertones (see pic with navy top above the row of these pants to see color difference), teal is a lovely soft teal (more blue than green, although it looks gray in some of my pics, it's not...it's just not vibrant bold teal) and black is a more true dark black than the fleece-lined Dragon Fit brand leggings (which I also tested which are pictured on far left of first pic).STYLE: I really liked both styles, thought they were comfortable and will wear both. I am more likely to wear the side-pocket style at home for easy phone reach, but less attractive because of the phone in the pocket look, while I'm more likely to use the waist pocket style at the gym.SIZING: I'm about 5'4" and 130lbs, and both sizes were stretchy enough to fit, however the SMALL was more comfortable on me. The ankles are fitted and both were long enough for me, however the Small ended about two inches above my ankle, while the Small size was about an inch longer.Overall, these are what they claim to be, buttery-soft and very comfortable leggings. Love the pocket options and the gusseted stitching on the crotch. Fabric and make feel high quality and I love the high waist on these as it helped my shirts lay nicely. Overall, I think the size chart is good guidance, but colors need renaming.. I'd highly recommend to anyone looking for very comfortable and durable leggings.

One of the best cotton knit yoga capris I've ever worn. These are a fantastic casual mostly cotton Capri. I am 5'7 tall and I have a long torso and short legs. Most of the time capris look more like ankle pants on me but these actually are somewhere in between which is nice. They come down to about mid calf. These are not thick sweatpants but a nice thicker cotton. I would still feel comfortable wearing these in spring summer and fall without feeling like I was going to sweat to death. They have a waistband with a drawstring to fit you comfortably. I weigh 189 lb and got the extra large and they fit perfectly. The pockets are nice and deep and there was a surprise pocket on my right hip on the inside. I'm not exactly sure what I would use this for but it's nice to know that there's one there. Maybe that's where I keep my quarter for the grocery cart at Aldi's? Did I mention how comfortable these things are? I would love another pair or two in other colors to toss into my everyday mommy wardrobe. They washed well with a little shrinkage but nothing extraordinary. I think these are a good value and will absolutely buy them again.

Soft, decent leg length for taller people The first time I tried these on (unwashed) they were super soft and very tight. No seams on the outside of legs which was nice. I went for a 2 1/2 mile run and the longer I wore them, the more comfortable they became, until they just felt like a second skin. Nice. However, I would not wear these to the gym on leg day because they felt like the rear would split open. The rear was not see-through but under garment is absolutely noticeable, leaving clear lines. I did not feel secure squatting down or doing leg curls but fine for upper body workouts and running. Took them off and washed them then hung to dry... not so soft any longer. Didn't want to put them in dryer out of fear they might shrink and now they feel kind of rough. Color is unique, price point is a bit high. I'm 5'9" and they reach my ankles. High waist is comfortable to wear for a decent length workout.

SO PERFECT!! I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Its regular high waist not too high and the crossover makes them show more stomach than regular straight waist pants which is so flattering. The waist isn’t too tight. I probably could’ve fit the small. But I’m happy with the XS and will definitely order more in a different color. I’ll probably gift these for my sister around the holidays as well. I have an event coming up that I needed these for and ordered them later than I should’ve however they came in a day earlier than expected which was awesome. There are no holes, weird spots or crooked stitching. I could definitely wear these to bed they’re so comfortable. I really am so so happy with them and can’t wait to wear them. I already know I will get so many wears out of these. I would say they fit true to size and are nice and stretchy even though they fit snug. If your questioning weather to get them or not I would say go for it! They are PERFECT!

Solid leggings, but unflattering on me My measurements: waist 24", hips 38". I'm 5'8" and have a long torso (my rise is long but I don't have much space under my chest, so I need a high rise but it can easily overwhelm me). I've got hip dips and not a lot of upper glutes. I was searching for a pair of flare leggings that had no front seam, flattering glute seams, a long inseam, and that aren't $70; these are the ONLY ones I've found so far. I ordered the XS 34" inseam. The good things: I like how high rise they are! Not in love with the cross waist, but it's not bad. The bottom of the V comes down to just above my belly button. They changed the gusset design so now it's a small triangle. They're a great length. The fabric is a performance material that's cool to the touch, super stretchy, smooth and kind of soft. No loose seams. It's got some weight to it, so I would size down if you're in between sizes just to avoid any sliding around. No show underwear would be best with it but the fabric isn't super thin, so you could get away with regular underwear depending on the thickness. I'm not wearing no-show underwear in the pics. Now onto the bad... I really don't think these are very flattering on me. The design of the crotch gusset means that my butt just eats up the lower part of the back seam, in a way that doesn't look intentional, and I don't have enough upper glutes to do the same to the top part so it ends up looking weird and kinda making my butt look smaller?? Now I like me some scrunch leggings, I even like regular leggings to fit my glutes, but for some reason these just look a little off. I'm returning this color. I'm debating whether I'll keep the black pair and just wear oversized stuff over it. If I could rate this 3.5 stars I would, I don't want to give it 3 stars but I don't like it enough for 4 stars personally. If I keep the black and end up changing my mind I'll come back and update this review.Overall good leggings, everything except the back is fine, just not for me.

SO PERFECT!! I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Solid leggings, but unflattering on me My measurements: waist 24", hips 38". I'm 5'8" and have a long torso (my rise is long but I don't have much space under my chest, so I need a high rise but it can easily overwhelm me). I've got hip dips and not a lot of upper glutes. I was searching for a pair of flare leggings that had no front seam, flattering glute seams, a long inseam, and that aren't $70; these are the ONLY ones I've found so far. I ordered the XS 34" inseam. The good things: I like how high rise they are! Not in love with the cross waist, but it's not bad. The bottom of the V comes down to just above my belly button. They changed the gusset design so now it's a small triangle. They're a great length. The fabric is a performance material that's cool to the touch, super stretchy, smooth and kind of soft. No loose seams. It's got some weight to it, so I would size down if you're in between sizes just to avoid any sliding around. No show underwear would be best with it but the fabric isn't super thin, so you could get away with regular underwear depending on the thickness. I'm not wearing no-show underwear in the pics. Now onto the bad... I really don't think these are very flattering on me. The design of the crotch gusset means that my butt just eats up the lower part of the back seam, in a way that doesn't look intentional, and I don't have enough upper glutes to do the same to the top part so it ends up looking weird and kinda making my butt look smaller?? Now I like me some scrunch leggings, I even like regular leggings to fit my glutes, but for some reason these just look a little off. I'm returning this color. I'm debating whether I'll keep the black pair and just wear oversized stuff over it. If I could rate this 3.5 stars I would, I don't want to give it 3 stars but I don't like it enough for 4 stars personally. If I keep the black and end up changing my mind I'll come back and update this review.Overall good leggings, everything except the back is fine, just not for me.

These pants are smooth, sleek dramatically flared yoga pants. I am 5’10” & 195 lbs and have spent years and a small fortune seeking the perfect bootcut/flare legging. THE SEARCH IS OVER! The flares fit through the legs like regular leggings to right below the knee then flare rather dramatically . This cut is very flattering, as is the crossover waist with a true high rise. The only drawback is that the waist rolls when I’m seated, but this is more a flaw of my own figure than of these pants (I need to tighten up in the abdominal department.) As for the material, it is pet hair resistant, sleek but not shiny, and feels like a perfectly stretchy dream. Let’s not forget the length option. The 34” length is perfection. Don’t wait another second! You need these!

Too high waisted Fit: These high waisted yoga pants are extremely high waisted, particularly in the back. The crosswaist is very flattering, but it was so high waisted that I had no more than 2 inches between the back of my bra and the top of the waistband. Every time I moved the top of the waistband would roll down which is uncomfortable. Material: The material is slick, rather than brushed like the Sunzel leggings. The outlines of the pockets are visible, which I also did not like. Sizing: I got a medium. Waist: 29in, Glutes: 39in. I have a short torso. At 5'1" I could wear these with sneakers and probably just have the hems graze the ground. Overall, I'd love to see a normal waist, brushed material yoga pant in multiple inseam lengths with pockets that are sewn onto the fabric (like on the Sunzel pocket leggings) so the pocket doesn't create a lump on the hip area.

I have other flare leggings that are more flattering, but I think these are not quite as much so because of the pockets. I think the price is decently fair (maybe a bit high? $27.99 as of the time of this review).

The good, the bad and the rest Actually these are a 4.5 star. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. I went for the XLarge with 30" inseam. I really like these leggings. They are very smooth and soft, fitting nicely. These have been through the washer and dryer a couple times with no issues. The seams all seem well sewn, but I'm not a fan of the shiny thread that goes down the outer leg. It kinda feels like it cheapens them and keeps me from being able to wear in a say a work environment or an evening out with the hubs. Otherwise, they are very comfortable on. And I really liked that they offer different inseam options! Why do more womens pants not do this??? For that alone it bumped the star back up. The flare at the ankle is a bit much, but I can easily cut it down to my tastes. And that's all that is, my tastes, not a complaint on the pants. But just wanting to state that the fabric and cut otherwise is worth altering. The waist band has no extra elastic or pull cord in it, just relying on the compression on the fabric to hold them up. So far it hasn't been and issue as the elasticity of the fabric is good. The pockets are great and can easily hold my camera. Overall the only detriment is the shiny thread, but if you only want to work out in them they would be fine. I can comfortably recommend. Peace and soft pants

These flare leggings have a clean design. The material is soft and smooth, lightweight and have a slinky feel. The leggings are made of 80% nylon and 20% spandex with some stretch. They are medium in thickness and feel sturdy. I like that it is not see-through. The quality is very good, and did not change after washing. I do like the v-shaped waist and it is double layered for extra support. It provides a flattering look. The two pockets are a nice addition and are pretty deep. However, you can't really put anything bulky in them as the items will bulge out due to how tight fitting the leggings are. I am 5’1” and on the petite slender side and I ordered the Small size. I typically wear X-Small, but I've ordered from Sunzel before and their sizing is made smaller and tighter, so Small was a slightly better fit for me though still a tad tight. I recommend going a size or two up from your normal size. The length on these is a bit long on me even with shoes. The bottom of the pants is more bell shaped and pretty wide rather than bootcut flare. Overall, these are nice leggings with a clean design but do have a tight fit. If this is your style then these are worth consideration but I recommend going up a size or two. At the time of review, this is $35.99.

Better than lululemon in my opinion I can't rave enough about Sunzel leggings! Seriously, they've completely won me over, surpassing even my old favorites from Lululemon. Okay, let's get real here. Lululemon? Yeah, they're nice and all, but let's talk about durability. My Lululemon leggings? They ended up looking like a cat scratched them after a few washes. But not my Sunzel leggings! They've held up like champs, no pilling in sight. And can we talk about sizing? Being on the shorter side, finding flared leggings that actually fit without dragging on the ground? Near impossible. But Sunzel? They offer the different length options which I love. I’m 5 foot so I went with the shortest inseam option, and let me tell you, they fit like a dream! No more dragging, no more DIY hemming. The moment I slipped into these leggings, I knew I'd found something special. The fabric? Oh, it's like wearing clouds. So soft and comfy, yet supportive in all the right places. Whether I'm running errands or working out, these leggings have become my new best friend. So, if you're tired of sacrificing comfort and durability for style, do yourself a favor and give Sunzel leggings a shot. Seriously, you won't regret it! I'm already planning my next purchase because one pair? Definitely not enough. Trust me, these leggings are a game-changer!

Super soft and comfortable These are the best! I decided to order these because I am only 5’0 and they had the 28” length. They are incredibly comfortable and stretchy while also fitting my body. They are thick and not transparent at all. I really like how flattering the shape is. I will definitely be wearing these a lot. The material is super soft

4’10 Woman Approved!!! I am super short with an even shorter inseam, and carry all of my weight in my lower quadrant. This is the first pair of yoga pants/flare leggings that fit me perfectly AND didn’t skim the ground at all!!! I received these the day before I was coaching a state wrestling tournament all weekend, so I didn’t get to try these in until I was at the hotel. I’m glad I took that risk, because they ended up being perfect!!! I needed something professional, comfortable, NOT SEE THROUGH when I am crouching on the mat with a literal stadium audience behind me, but still stretchy enough to fit all of my lower half, flattering, affordable, and didn’t drag…and these leggings nailed all of it.

Great for the Price! I haven’t worn this style of pants since middle school, but these are making me a believer again! Nice ultra-high rise & great fit. I am 5’8 and ordered the 32 inch and I could’ve done an 30 inch in seam. Other reviews say the material is thin, but I didn’t think anything of it! So happy to have received these!

Comfortable pants The material is very nice I probably could’ve sized down and got a medium since it is stretchy and the crotch is a bit long for me. For reference I am a 10/12 and 5’4. These will definitely be my go to pants for working out and lounging around the house!

Best stretchy pants I've tried in a while Black XL - 32" inseam. These pants come in a nice medium to thick weight fabric that's very soft to the touch. The stretch is amazing, and the high waist ensures that you won't be uncomfortable with binding at the hips or waist. The pant legs are in a single layer of fabric, while the waistband is folded into a double layer. The seams are strong with multi-needle stitching. I love the feature of an elongated gusset insert running at the inseam and crotch - I think it looks sporty and probably adds to the comfortable fit and flexibility of the garment. The flare at the hemline looks cute, and makes a nice silouette. I was so glad to see that they offered different pant lengths. The 32" inseam was perfect for me at 5ft - 7 1/2in, as you can see the length just hits the floor with flat sandals. If you want to wear these with platforms or thick sneakers, think about getting a longer inseam. I was able to pull the pants up higher and lower to adjust the length though. I really appreciate a brand that caters to their customers by offering options to get a perfect fit. I would defintiely recommend these. Love them!

Got these leggings to wear casually, and they are soooo comfortable. I already own a pair of the Aerie crossover flared leggings and the material on these pants feel notably higher quality. The waist on these leggings is seamless which is a must for for me. The fabric is very smooth and soft. I see some complaints about the thinness of the fabric but personally it's not a detractor for me; these leggings aren't really meant to be heavy compression, they're more like the Lululemon Nulu fabric used in the Align leggings. In fact, I would still consider them to be thicker than Align leggings and the amount of hold feels just right. They're also totally squat proof. I’m generally size 6/5ft5, I got the Medium 30” inseam as I have relatively short legs for my height. The fit is great! Though if you have slightly longer legs I think you would need a longer inseam. These are super flattering and the quality is amazing, definitely recommend!

The leggings came very quickly (2 days). The size was spot on. They do feel like quality leggings. Squat proof 100% and the stay snug to your waist. Will be buying more colours once they are available in 28” inseam

I like how the fabric feels a little more substantial in the Sunzel Mini Flare Leggings than other more opaque nulu fabric leggings. They are still lightweight and comfortable for both the gym or running errands as well as walking my dog. I appreciate the gentle flare that looks stylish without being too wide, so no hindrance when using elliptical or bike. I found the fit to be true to size and the waistband stretchy and smooth hitting high on my waist without rolling or sliding down when doing exercises or yoga poses. I am 5’4″ and typically wear a 30″ inseam and these fit great with my sneakers. The cost is very reasonable for the quality and durability of these leggings which wash and wear very well. I found they don’t pill, shrink or stretch too much after wearing. The fabric feels buttery soft and fits nicely.

I could have gotten away with a medium but because im shorter and have wider hips/ thicker thighs I like that the large has enough tummy control and it doesn't fold at the top like a lot of brands do. I would recommend and would buy again!

Good material, fit was a bit large, dye marks on front & back My measurements: waist 24", hips 38". I'm 5'8", have a very high rise, and usually need longer inseams for leggings. I ordered an XS. The material is smooth, very stretchy, and not irritating, but they were looser than I would expect from an XS (I do not like any looseness in the waist). I would size down if you're in between or on the lower end of your size range. No loose seams. There were large dye lines on both the front and back of the leggings, not creases from being folded and packaged, actual dark dye marks. You can see them in the picture, but they actually looked worse in real life. There was a big line of dye from the top of the waistband to the crotch on the front as well. I'd order like 20 pairs if they fixed the slightly loose fit and the quality control, but I'm returning these.

Do yourself a favor and buy like 10 of these The pictures don't do these leggings justice. These leggings were the first time I finally felt confident again after 2-3 years of feeling disgusted by my body. When I first took them out of the package, I thought they looked like a child's size, that there was no way they were going to fit. I maybe could've gone up one size, but honestly they fit great. I risked a size large when I'm usually at least an xl-5xl (depending on the brand's sizing), and I'm glad I did. They're so stretchy, yet so solid. They're holding & shaping in all the right places, and super comfortable as far as tight pants go. Great on men / masc-presenting folx too, bc I was at Costco the other day, and some random lady complimented my legs / butt while I was wearing these lol. I've also noticed that these hardly hold any dog fur hostage, unlike some of my other leggings, which are constantly covered in dog fur. 11/10 would recommend. Definitely buying more of these. Hip measurement: ~45" Waist measurement: ~35"

Super comfortable I already owned the regular flare cross waist leggings and the mini flared leggings. So when I saw this brand posting a giveaway on IG for the mini flare WITH POCKETS I jumped all over that. In the past I’ve sized down in Sunzel products because they’re so stretchy and I love compression. However I feel they are TTS. Sunzel is my go to Amazon legging! I ordered my normal size in these but was nervous because one time I ordered pocket leggings in my normal size and they were too small. But I feel they have made changes and they’re TTS now. I love how deep the pockets are and can fit my iPhone 13 Pro Max. I love that the seams are flat locked. I have never had an issue with them not being squat proof either. My only issue is my fault. I ordered them in a 30” inseam and I needed them a little longer. It’s not THAT noticeable imo. Especially when standing still. But I noticed when I walked, and my thighs rubbed together, they did come up higher on the ankle. But it does have good vertical stretch, so I was able to make them work. If they come out with a longer inseam I will 100% be ordering them. Don’t get me wrong, I’m still going to wear these. But just be aware they’re a 30” inseam.

Sensory-friendly- Bought 2 pairs! I’m pretty sensitive to fabrics so leggings can sometimes be a nightmare. LOUNGEWEAR - Not lulu but great quality for the price I’ve purchased these in black and wine colors last year for $30, and saw the hot pink ($14) and forest green ($19) go on sale so I tried them and I’m keeping them. The forest green is nothing like the pictures as other reviews said. More of a teal/green (see pics), and significantly lighter of a shade than the photo. Pink is spot on neon pink you’d expect. For the price, they’re both perfect to lounge around the house in. At the end of the day lulu aligns are $98 and for loungewear the no one will see, these are a great alternative. Comparing these to lulu aligns (used sell their clothes so very familiar with the brand) - similar buttery soft feeling, they equally pick up just as much lint and dog hair, and they both pill in the inner thighs (maybe if you have a thigh gap that won’t happen in either brand) but lulu aligns long term also just pill all over from washing (to be fair, some pairs I’ve had 10+ years and you can trade them in to the store for credit towards a new pairs). I haven’t owned Sunzel long enough to say the same, but I imagine they will too based on the fabric. Lulu leggings that are NOT aligns don’t pill like this - for anyone interested in taking the leap. They are different in the crotch and waistband though - comparing a M Sunzel to a 6 lulu (5’10” 145~150 lbs for reference) - the Sunzel has noticeably more material in the crotch. It doesn’t stand out as much in the black, but in the colored leggings, especially when you sit, you’ll see extra fabric bunched up down there vs. lulu I’ve never noticed significant “bunching” in any color. And while there’s extra crotch material, the waist band is tighter in Sunzel. Positives are you could probably do a high intensity work out in these (lulu aligns are aimed for lounging and yoga, not running or high impact) but these stay up better. The negative being you might want to size up if you’re using these for lounging. I’m between a 4/6 in jeans and typically buy a S/M in non-numbered clothing. I carry more weight in my legs than stomach so for me they’re comfy. If you’re more of a 6/8 or are slightly more top heavy with thin legs or evenly spread, I’d honestly recommend trying the large just to have that comfort in the waist band for lounging. If you use these for working out, but them in your normal size. I’d definitely need a small if I wore these to the gym or for a run.

I haven’t owned Sunzel long enough to say the same, but I imagine they will too based on the fabric. Lulu leggings that are NOT aligns don’t pill like this - for anyone interested in taking the leap. They are different in the crotch and waistband though - comparing a M Sunzel to a 6 lulu (5’10” 145~150 lbs for reference) - the Sunzel has noticeably more material in the crotch. It doesn’t stand out as much in the black, but in the colored leggings, especially when you sit, you’ll see extra fabric bunched up down there vs. lulu I’ve never noticed significant “bunching” in any color. And while there’s extra crotch material, the waist band is tighter in Sunzel. Positives are you could probably do a high intensity work out in these (lulu aligns are aimed for lounging and yoga, not running or high impact) but these stay up better. The negative being you might want to size up if you’re using these for lounging. I’m between a 4/6 in jeans and typically buy a S/M in non-numbered clothing. I carry more weight in my legs than stomach so for me they’re comfy. If you’re more of a 6/8 or are slightly more top heavy with thin legs or evenly spread, I’d honestly recommend trying the large just to have that comfort in the waist band for lounging. If you use these for working out, but them in your normal size. I’d definitely need a small if I wore these to the gym or for a run.

After wearing them throughout the day, it felt fine. Material has high stretch and great tummy control. Great for workouts (squat proof), helps keep the abdomen tight for the heavy lifting. No issues rolling down. Stayed in place. I knocked off one star because it does catch lint very easily, so I air-dry them only and wash them inside out. I had to buy a second set, I really like them. UPDATE- I loved these so much previously, so I decided to buy some more. They CHANGED! This is NOT the same material or design. The XL now feels like a 2X, and they are so thin they feel like pantyhose or leggings that go under a dress. I no longer recommend and I’m SO disappointed. I now have to find my gym pants elsewhere. Ugh.

Good leggings I looked through several reviews before I bought these...so I wanted to make sure that I wrote an honest and accurate review to help any other looking to buy. I am 5'7 and roughly 160lbs after having 4 children...I bought a size large and am pleased with the fit. These are not miracle working pants, you won't look like the woman in the pictures unless you have the same body as her lol...with that being said, they are more flattering than your regular every day leggings. They give your booty a nice curve and the angled waistline helps give you the appearance of a slightly slimmer waist. I have thick thighs and child bearing hips and definitely could not fit into a medium. The large fits well with minimum belly bulge, I might be inclined to try an Xtra large if I order again. The material is thick enough to not be see-through and stretchy enough to be comfortable. Overall, a good purchase.

LONG SWEATPANTS These are super comfy sweatpants and I am surprised how long they are! I’m 6’ tall and these come to the floor! They are plenty roomy, pretty wide legs actually. But they’re a great pair of sweatpants and plenty warm, I might get some more! They fit true to size.

XXL not very long, or wide, but SOFT I REALLY wanted to love these, as they are pretty thick, super soft, and Borahae purple. But they are getting returned for a variety of reasons. I'm 5'8" and 195 lbs. The pants weren't tight in the butt (and mine is big), but they weren't loose enough either. Most importantly, they are made lowrise ONLY IN THE BACK, so if you have any butt at all, it IMMEDIATELY starts to slide down and give you peek-a-crack. My backside photo was taken less than one minute after I pulled the waistline up as high as I can without hurting my lady parts. The front of the pants folds under my belly instantly, but I'll take the blame for that one. The other part that was a no for me is, they are BARELY long enough for me, and I'm not particularly tall. If I were 5'10" or taller, they'd be highwaters on me. The lack of length is already unflattering, and I'm only 5'8". For XXL, I would expect longer in the legs (and butt) unless it's labeled as XXL Petite. Notes that didn't impact me returning these: The only label on or in them is on the inside side seam near the pocket. I posted a photo. And the waistband seam is only ok, folded over, but not double folded. So you can see the fabric cut, and I fear the seam could come loose after a few washes. The band is soft and bunchy, though, and the drawstrings seem quality. Cheers and good luck in looking for pants, especially if you need purple ones!

good; not amazing I bought a size Large, grey; I am 5'7", 130lbs, 26" waist, 32" hips. These fit pretty well. They sit right on my hips without me tying the drawstring, which I like. They bunch up a little at my ankles; if I tie them so that they are high-waisted, they reach my ankles without being too long or too short. They have good pockets and seem fairly well-made. The inside texture is not exactly soft, but still comfortable. These would not keep you very warm in cold weather because they are not fleece. These are thin but not too thin; probably only thin because there is no inner fleece lining. over all, I like them; however, they are more of a loose, warm weather lounging pair of sweatpants, which is good, but I am still on the lookout for a pair of solid, thicker, and baggy sweatpants ;)

Def wouldn’t call these baggy, but they’re nice I’ve been wearing these sweats in my rotation at home since August, and they’re not my top pick, but they are pretty nice. To be fair, I like a sweatpant with a really loose fit, especially in the waist. I’m pretty average sized (5’4”, 150lbs, athletic, longer torso and shorter legs), and I would not call these baggy by any means. The waist is definitely tight enough to where you can feel that it’s there; these are more the type of sweatpants that I wouldn’t feel at all uncomfortable wearing out of the house. They’ve got a cinched ankle, a cute tie in the waistband, and good pockets. If you prefer some sweats that hug your bum and won’t slide down at all, or if you are a waif queen, these will definitely be a nice option for you! They are quite soft and comfy in terms of material. As a side note, I know the selling page says to hand wash, but I’ve been tossing these in the laundry with everything else and haven’t had a problem. Like with all my drawstring pants, I tie a little bunny knot with the strings so they don’t get lost in the waistband. The price is right, and for some, these might be the perfect sweaties!

They are decent These are workable sweats. For a teen, they are exactly the style everyone is wearing. For an adult (like me, wanting cozy), I wouldn't probably get these again. The inside is fairly comfortable but the outside is rougher than expected. The drawstring doesn't really do anything so size yourself appropriately because there's no cinching. I got a medium, and wear a 8/10 typically and these fit perfectly, a bit baggy where they need to be and waistband is just right. They come to my ankles, I'm 5'8". My biggest gripe apart from fabric is the ankles are tightttt. Like on my mind constantly because it's annoying. Leaves dents in my skin. This might be the great divide between teens and adults, if ankles are somehow smaller on younger kids wearing the same waist size as me. Otherwise stitches etc are good, but the overall product is noticeably not on the same level as sporty name brands.

Some fit better than others. I purchased medium pairs of the long rainbow striped pants and the short double white striped pants. I love how the rainbow pair fits while the other pair fits more like leggings. I am 5'6 and 135-140 LBS, I have wide hips and a small waist and these sweats are very flattering to a more hour glass figure. However, if my waist was just an inch wider the waistband would be too tight (which could be problematic for square or apple shaped figures). In general, I suggest sizing up, especially if you want a baggy fit or if you carry more weight around your tummy rather than your butt.

Take me back to Y2K!! I purchased these to wear to a concert: Tori Kelly's "Purple Skies" tour. The album screamed Y2K vibes and... hello, PURPLE!! The fit: The pants fit a little bit snug around my waist. I ordered a Medium, as I'm typically a medium in leggings and sweatpants. Even though they fit snug, I don't know that I would have sized up because everywhere else, the fit was just right.. any baggier I wouldn't have loved the look as much. They come just above my bellybutton, so they'd look nice with a cropped top as well. I'm 5'7" and they hit right above my ankle; however, depending on the shoe you wear, it won't look like you're about to wade through a flash flood. You can also loosen or tighten the draw string at the ankles. The color: These pants are VERY purple. Less of a royal purple color, more like a deep lilac; which I quite like (see the second pic for the true color). So, keep that in mind :) The quality: Without a doubt, these are some pretty well made pants for an amazon find. It's always hit or miss for me when shopping for clothing.. sometimes things are so thin and very see through; but, the fabric used for these pants are on par with any decently priced cargo's from a traditional retailer. I've washed and dried them with no shrinkage or color bleeding and very minimal wrinkles. All of the pockets are functional!

My mom is also 5’6, and I’m not sure how much she weighs, but she’s not that much bigger than me. I didn’t really like these only because when I went to sit down, the back had no stretch. Adorable! Great purchase. So I was really confused what size to order because some said order up and others said order down. For reference, I am 5'9 and 125 lbs. I have smaller thighs and often pants can be baggy in that area so I ordered the xs. (grey #1) BIG mistake. Way too small. These have stretch, but not that much. Size chart didn't help. My hips don't lie : ) I love pants made of this type of fabric (tops too for that matter). It's like there's a subtle wavy wrinkle built into it if that makes sense. The fabric is cool to wear and has a flowy feeling when you walk and it is so easy to launder. I'm almost 5'5" but my torso is long so my legs are on the shorter side and length is almost always an issue for me when buying pants or maxi skirts. But, when I saw on the size chart that the inseam was between 27.5-28", I was excited to order these pants! I usually wear a 10 - I'm about 38-34-43 - this put me between medium and large on the size chart but the hip measurements were so generous, I decided to go with the medium, and that turned out to be the right decision. When I buy pants, it is common for there to be an empty pooch pocket of fabric in my belly area - my backside and hips have no trouble filling the pants out, and while I am thankful that I don't fill out the belly area of these pants, they would look SO much better if I could slim down my thighs and hips. (And, my short legs certainly don't do my hips any favors.) If I could slim down a bit, I would love to have these in lighter colors. Pricing is pretty good, and I don't have to have them hemmed so that's a bonus!

Comfortable Fashionable Palazzo Pants Great choice for a pair of palazzo pants. I'm usually a size small but my 5'9" frame requires longer pant legs and this medium pair worked fine. I love the elastic waist in the back which gives the comfort I want when wearing a pair of pants all day. The extra leg material, which is its style, can be supported by wearing a pair of heels so your frame won't be overwhelmed with the extra material. The material is comfortable and sturdy, thick and with a standard feel, as in not scratchy to the skin. The wrinkles were easy to remove with a steamer and the material appears washer friendly.

Pretty Color; Not Amazing I had higher hopes for this one. I’m 192 pounds and 5’6. I got a size large. What I like about it: the color is beautiful. The fabric is almost silky and super soft. It has pockets. What I don’t love about it: the fit was a bit weird. Because of the waist band, I expected it to hold in your tummy a bit but it doesn’t. The pants are not see through but the fabric was thin enough that if I pulled up the waist band to be flattering on my waist, you could see my cellulite on my thighs. If you’re smaller and don’t have cellulite, these would probably work great for you! The problem is if I would have sized down one, the waist band probably would have been more flattering but then the pants would have been too tight for my liking on my thighs. All together: for me personally: they’re okay, but not super flattering. I probably won’t be wearing them out of the house, but they’re super comfortable for at home, they’re loose on the legs and it’s a silkyish stretchy material.

Great comfort for curvy figures! I had these on my Amazon Wish List, and my sister bought them for me as a gift. They are even better than I expected! I have a curvy figure with a small waist and larger hips/bottom (plus post-menopause belly), and these fit perfectly. I chose medium, my usual size in pants. The waistband is snug without being tight, and the length grazes the floor with shoes. (For reference, I am just under 5'5", 152 lbs.) These pants are comfortable enough for me to wear when teaching yoga but also dressy enough looking for me to wear to work. I'm thinking about getting more colors! UPDATE, 8/8/24 I've now owned these for about 8 months; I've probably worn them 6-8x. I am still really happy with the comfort and the fit. However, I've noticed that after washing, there is some pilling to the fabric - I can feel it when I run my hands down the front. I had thought about getting these in gray as well, but now I'm hesitant because I'm not sure how they will hold up.

Super comfy I love the fabric thickness, quality and color. I’m 5’2”, 115 lbs and ordered a small, they fit great but I’d probably go up to a medium next time because I do feel like they shrank a little. Tag in pants says dry clean only (which I’m not going to do) I washed on delicate cycle with cold water and put them in the dryer which is why I will size up due to slight shrinkage. I love the pockets, draw string at waist and loose fit. I’d absolutely order again in additional colors. Two things: they arrived in a ziplock bag with zero tape to secure it and the pants had a slight “new fabric” odor but that went away after being washed once.

Two sizes tried, like them both I’ve chosen boyfriends with less effort and time then it’s taken to make a decision on these pants… I’ve gone back and forth for three days trying these on to see which size I like best. This is my fourth revision of my review. And, honestly, the jury is still out, because both sizes I tried fit great, albeit different. My stats: age 45, 5’5”, 170lbs, 43-36-43, 36F/38E, flat butt with chub on the flanks (back area of hips just below the waist), normal size bought on amazon L/XL. What I ordered: Small (in white) and medium (in black). The deets: Fabric is a great weight; feels thick but not heavy; says 70% rayon/30% linen on tag. White is on the sheer side, but no more so than other white linen pants would be. The medium fit like a true baggy harem pant. Lots of material around the hip/legs/butt area. The waist was a little loose before tying the drawstring. The abundance of fabric did add some bulk or perceived girth to my hips, but I found the same bagginess to be flattering to the hip/butt area since it wasn’t hugging the spots I am self conscious about. I do find that I am constantly adjusting the tie depending on whether I am sitting or standing. The small fit was perfect at the waist; the elastic still had plenty of stretch and fit just right at rest. (At rest, the waist was about two inches smaller than the medium.) There was significantly less fabric around the hip and butt area. This lended itself to a more tailored, less frumpy look. However, I noticed that because the pant has less of a dropped crotch and less fabric in the booty, I felt like my cheeks were eating the material; I noticed the square shape of my backside more, particularly the chunk at the back of hip. Length is a little shorter on white. Conclusion: I think these are a great FP-inspired pant. The closest of all the brands on here to the original inspiration. The problem for me is that I still can’t decide which size I like more. The difference in amount of material from one size to another really does change the whole look of the pant, and not necessarily in a bad way. I think the looser fitting medium is great for lounging or running errands, paired with a crop tank or even oversized top; much like the look of the FP version. The more tailored through the hip/tummy/butt look of the small makes the style a little more work-appropriate, and still comfy enough for lounging, etc. It really DOES depend on what look you're going for. Would I be crazy to keep both sizes?!

Perfect summer pants I love these pants! They are drawstring and elastic wait. The fabric is a linen blend- super light and flowy. They are perfect beach pant for a bathing suit cover up or just to wear around when it’s hot- they feel like wearing air! They feel like a great dupe for a popular boho brand which costs 3x more. They have washed well. I ordered several different versions and colors of this pant from different makers, and for me these were the best! I love the river blue color- so summery. They have a nice pocket and pleated pocket detail on the leg. They do have a dropped crotch kind of harem style, which I love, and is probably what makes them so comfortable. I’m usually between a medium and a large, and I’m wearing the medium. The large was huge, and I maybe could have taken a small. I am also tall, 5’10’ and they are still not super cropped on me. So depending on what look you want, you may want to size down. I also tried the army green color but it was a little bit too light for me- I loved this blue. If you are looking for super relaxed, flow, beach vibe- give them a go!

Good set for lounging at home I am an apple shaped, mid-sized curvy girl, 5'5" with waist 38, Hips 46, band 35, bust 42, weight about 195lbs, Bra size 38DDD. I am right on the edge between a size large and an XL, short torso, average inseam. The size chart doesn't have a hip measurement which is what I would have used to choose a size, so I went with the large. The fabric is pretty stretchy, so they fit fine but I probably would have preferred an XL to prevent pulling at the pockets. The fabric is a tad thin, so personally wouldn't wear them out of the house, but they are great for pajama/lounge pants. It is nice to have the pockets to slip my phone in. They are pretty long on me, so I think they could work even for some of the taller girls. Overall I think this is a good set for the price.
