Don't love this version due to the scrunch, but great customer service. I bought these after seeing lots of girls in our Fit Finds FB group gush about them. I was never going to spend the money on the WS x GS collection (if I could even snag a pair before they sold out) but loved the look of them, especially the scrunch and laser detail on the back, so I was pretty excited to see this dupe show up and get great reviews. To be transparent, I absolutely adore leggings and have tons of pairs from Amazon, AliExpress, and many other more expensive brands. I wear them every chance I get because I think they are really flattering and comfortable as hell. Aligns or the Amazon Hawthorne/HeyNutz ones that are very similar are my daily go-tos, and I like Wunder Unders or similar slick things for the gym, so that will give you an idea of what I prefer. I'm pretty open to any brand or price, as long as they're great for the gym and/or comfortable and flattering, or just a good lounging pair. I'm normally a black leggings kind of girl but decided to branch out and try this gorgeous GREEN, and then later DANDELION BROWN, and both are super cute. I'd say the colors are true to the picture. They aren't the most cellulite friendly, but I expect that with pretty much any color that isn't black or patterned. I got a MEDIUM in the GREEN pair and a LARGE in the DANDELION BROWN pair. I have pretty consistently been a size medium in leggings for years, but I've recently gained some more weight, so I'm more on the true large side now. I'm 5'3" and I think my measurements are about 43-33-43 (bust-waist-hip) right now, unfortunately, and I'm a 10 or 12 in AE jeans, depending on the pair. That being said, both the medium and large fit me great and about the same, so take that into consideration when trying to determine your size. They are super stretchy. While I think these have potential, these two versions (aka the original with laser detail and newer without it) are just not my thing. Both pairs fit me great, but the scrunch completely stretched out and made me look like I had a slightly wrinkly butt-crack, which is not really the look I was going for. It's hard to tell in my pictures, but I promise, that's what it looked like. I think the scrunch is just too weak and a big butt stretches it out. I've been told that Tom Tiger plans on fixing it, so I have high hopes for future versions! They do help give a little glute separation, so some may love that it's way more subtle than something like the Alphalete Amplifies with their very intense scrunch. I guess I just wanted something a little more in-between. Also, for those of you that hate WAISTBAND SEAMS in leggings, like me, you should note that both versions do have a seam there, but NO ELASTIC BAND! It's just a thin sewn seam around the top of the waistband. I would definitely prefer they find a way to just make it a folded material waistband and do away with the seam, but it doesn't bother me too much, just cuts into my fat a little bit. Due to my short torso, I also have issues with leggings rolling a lot on my waist, and these definitely rolled a bit. I did enjoy the cute laser cutout on the back and the general shapes of the seams, but I found the lack of a front seam, and the higher seams of the front waistband, made my lower tummy area look pooched out. I unfortunately hold some weight there right now so just didn't feel confident in them. If you are lean in your tummy area, that wouldn't be an issue for you, obviously. I wish that waist seam sat a bit lower to give me some compression, but I don't know how that would look. The other thing I didn't love about these is the material. I'm not great at describing this sort of thing, but these are like a super thin but slick material, and they honestly feel a bit cheap. They aren't rough, just not my favorite. I also had some of those line dye marks on the butt and a few on the front and legs of both pairs, as you may be able to spy in the photo of the Green pair. I come across this issue with random leggings on occasion. I assume it has something to do with how they are stored, maybe the dye process, and/or them being folded for extended periods of time in different temperatures so the dye settles in those folds. We'll see if they go away with a wash. Despite the things I didn't love, I do still recommend everyone try these for themselves, and like I said, I have high hopes for future versions. I really like the company, as they are very interested in customer feedback and actually listen to critique. I highly recommend anyone that loves leggings add Tom Tiger (and their sister company, Aurolas) on Instagram and Facebook so you can interact with them. They are very responsive and open to suggestions. TLDR: I got a Medium in Green, Large in Dandelion Brown; both fit about the same. These have a sewn seam without an elastic band around the top of the waistband. The butt-scrunch doesn't really do much but help with some glute separation. The material is thin and kind of slick, feels a bit cheap; not my favorite. They are not cellulite friendly in these two colors, but who cares. While not my fav, I still recommend people try them to see how they feel! We all like different things. I also really like the company.

Don't love this version due to the scrunch, but great customer service. I bought these after seeing lots of girls in our Fit Finds FB group gush about them. I was never going to spend the money on the WS x GS collection (if I could even snag a pair before they sold out) but loved the look of them, especially the scrunch and laser detail on the back, so I was pretty excited to see this dupe show up and get great reviews. To be transparent, I absolutely adore leggings and have tons of pairs from Amazon, AliExpress, and many other more expensive brands. I wear them every chance I get because I think they are really flattering and comfortable as hell. Aligns or the Amazon Hawthorne/HeyNutz ones that are very similar are my daily go-tos, and I like Wunder Unders or similar slick things for the gym, so that will give you an idea of what I prefer. I'm pretty open to any brand or price, as long as they're great for the gym and/or comfortable and flattering, or just a good lounging pair. I'm normally a black leggings kind of girl but decided to branch out and try this gorgeous GREEN, and then later DANDELION BROWN, and both are super cute. I'd say the colors are true to the picture. They aren't the most cellulite friendly, but I expect that with pretty much any color that isn't black or patterned. I got a MEDIUM in the GREEN pair and a LARGE in the DANDELION BROWN pair. I have pretty consistently been a size medium in leggings for years, but I've recently gained some more weight, so I'm more on the true large side now. I'm 5'3" and I think my measurements are about 43-33-43 (bust-waist-hip) right now, unfortunately, and I'm a 10 or 12 in AE jeans, depending on the pair. That being said, both the medium and large fit me great and about the same, so take that into consideration when trying to determine your size. They are super stretchy. While I think these have potential, these two versions (aka the original with laser detail and newer without it) are just not my thing. Both pairs fit me great, but the scrunch completely stretched out and made me look like I had a slightly wrinkly butt-crack, which is not really the look I was going for. It's hard to tell in my pictures, but I promise, that's what it looked like. I think the scrunch is just too weak and a big butt stretches it out. I've been told that Tom Tiger plans on fixing it, so I have high hopes for future versions! They do help give a little glute separation, so some may love that it's way more subtle than something like the Alphalete Amplifies with their very intense scrunch. I guess I just wanted something a little more in-between. Also, for those of you that hate WAISTBAND SEAMS in leggings, like me, you should note that both versions do have a seam there, but NO ELASTIC BAND! It's just a thin sewn seam around the top of the waistband. I would definitely prefer they find a way to just make it a folded material waistband and do away with the seam, but it doesn't bother me too much, just cuts into my fat a little bit. Due to my short torso, I also have issues with leggings rolling a lot on my waist, and these definitely rolled a bit. I did enjoy the cute laser cutout on the back and the general shapes of the seams, but I found the lack of a front seam, and the higher seams of the front waistband, made my lower tummy area look pooched out. I unfortunately hold some weight there right now so just didn't feel confident in them. If you are lean in your tummy area, that wouldn't be an issue for you, obviously. I wish that waist seam sat a bit lower to give me some compression, but I don't know how that would look. The other thing I didn't love about these is the material. I'm not great at describing this sort of thing, but these are like a super thin but slick material, and they honestly feel a bit cheap. They aren't rough, just not my favorite. I also had some of those line dye marks on the butt and a few on the front and legs of both pairs, as you may be able to spy in the photo of the Green pair. I come across this issue with random leggings on occasion. I assume it has something to do with how they are stored, maybe the dye process, and/or them being folded for extended periods of time in different temperatures so the dye settles in those folds. We'll see if they go away with a wash. Despite the things I didn't love, I do still recommend everyone try these for themselves, and like I said, I have high hopes for future versions. I really like the company, as they are very interested in customer feedback and actually listen to critique. I highly recommend anyone that loves leggings add Tom Tiger (and their sister company, Aurolas) on Instagram and Facebook so you can interact with them. They are very responsive and open to suggestions. TLDR: I got a Medium in Green, Large in Dandelion Brown; both fit about the same. These have a sewn seam without an elastic band around the top of the waistband. The butt-scrunch doesn't really do much but help with some glute separation. The material is thin and kind of slick, feels a bit cheap; not my favorite. They are not cellulite friendly in these two colors, but who cares. While not my fav, I still recommend people try them to see how they feel! We all like different things. I also really like the company.

These have so much potential Edit: After a few washes the lines came out and they still haven’t ripped so I’ve added a star. My measurements are 25” waist and 36” hips and I bought a size small. These leggings could be so amazing but I was disappointed. They arrived covered in lines, which kind of look like the dye had run where the leggings creased. I exchanged them and the new pair have the same lines but not as bad so I kept them. They’re really thin, which personally I prefer, but I’m scared they’re going to rip every time I put them on. They’re soft and very comfortable to wear. The butt scrunch is invisible when you’re wearing them which is great! Not 100% sweat proof but held up pretty well. They’re already piling from hip thrusts so I don’t know how long they’ll last. These might not be suitable for any workout that includes lower body contact. Like I said, these have great potential but just didn’t pull through for me. Regardless, they’d make perfect lounge leggings.

Best leggings I own! These leggings are AMAZING! I ordered the eucalyptus color and it was matched the product photo almost exactly. These leggings are thin material but SQUAT PROOF! I love how “high rise” they are and the 7/8 length on my 5ft height are perfect. They are definitely soft and buttery feeling on the legs. Love Great quality, feels good on your skin. I’m 200lbs & ordered a large which fit true to size. Very durable in the gym I just ordered 3 more colors. They do not suck in the waist tho so if you’re looking for that go with something that has a thick waistband.

Great Leggings EXCEPT the waistband! I am a ride or die Tom Tiger fan and have their leggings and 3” inseam shorts in almost every color. These leggings are great. I just wish these had the same waistband as the shorts!

Super cute, but a little small I previously ordered a pair of their shorts in a medium and loved them so much I decided to try these leggings in a medium. Comfy & Flattering, But Slightly Long. These TOPYOGAS yoga pants are incredibly comfy & flattering. Made from high-quality, slick, athletic material, they feel like true workout gear. They fit true to size but are very form-fitting. At first glance, I thought they’d sent me the wrong size because they looked so small 😄, but they’re super stretchy & fit like compression wear; snug but not uncomfortably tight. They run a bit long, slightly dragging on the floor. If you're tall, they’d probably be the perfect length. I'm 5'6", and they just touch the floor. It's not enough to bother me, but I do have to lift them a little when I go outside to avoid dragging. Personally, I prefer my pants a bit long, so it’s no big deal. They also offer mild tummy-smoothing properties, making my "mommy pooch" less noticeable without feeling restrictive. The material almost feels magical 😆. They're easy to care for; machine washable on a cold, delicate cycle. I opted to hang dry to avoid any chance of shrinking & to keep the material smooth, though a low-heat tumble dry would probably work fine. Best of all, they have pockets! That’s rare for athletic pants, & it’s a feature I really appreciate. The fabric is thin & breathable, so I never feel too hot, and they’re not see-through, so I can wear them confidently with shorter tops. The only reason I deducted a star is for the length, which hides the flare at the bottom a bit. Overall, though, they’re a high-quality pair of yoga pants that fit & look great.

Cute but the waist is super-high I ordered the XL because the size chart said the inseam is 30"... and it is. But I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs and when I pull the very high waist down to where it feels comfortable on me, there's a lot bunching above the crotch. When I pull the waist all the way up, the pants are too short. If they were skinny cut, like leggings, the length would be fine. On me, the fit is wonky and they're not long enough. Maybe someone of average height look and feel great in these. They're really cute though, with snazzy looking pockets and seaming for tummy control.

Nice but has flaws When you think of good leggings, this is the material you want. They are very soft, thick, but not hot. After washing, they have not pilled. They fit my 13 yr old daughter perfect, size small. She is 5'4 and 115 lbs. They do have two lines running through one side on the front and the other side on the back. It is like the runs you would get in panty hose back in the day, except these aren't sheer and don't seem like they'll rip. I thought maybe it was a crease from long term folding but it doesn't wash out. My daughter doesn't care though so we are keeping them. I took one star off for the flaws.

Waist is too high. Small fits like xsmall These are called "high waisted" and they sure are. They are cutting into my ribcage, that's how high they are. They do NOT come below the bellybutton like they do on the model. (I'm 5'4, so maybe they'd fit like that if you are very tall, or maybe if you have a long torso.) I tried rolling the top down further, but that exposes the inside seam and it doesn't look right. The waist is also quite tight. I ordered my normal size (small) but it fits like an xsmall. It's too tight in the crotch, and shows more in front than it should. However I would not order up a size, because then it would be even "higher waisted" and too long in the leg. The leg length is long - the heels drag on the floor. However that is not more so than it looks on the model, so I'm ok with that part. The side tie is cute but it doesn't really tighten the waist itself. It's more just for show. The fabric is a nylon blend, and it's thick and comfortable. It's not shiny like some nylon blends can be, and I like this matte feel better. The solid gray is as shown on screen.

Versatile.. wear to Gym or to Dinner These pants remind me of some athletic pants I had years ago and I really loved them. But no, I didn’t order these for yoga or pilates, I’ll simply be wearing these in place of other slacks for fairly casual activities. I selected the speckled “charcoal” color which has a tweedy look. I can wear these with any number of sweaters and shirts, and will even be wearing them to dinners.The fabric is stretch and slick, and are quite fitted. These flare out to a bootcut style and the fabric flows with movement and is very soft. This is made with a wide panel at the waist which can be drawn up into puckers by a fabric cord on the left side, or can be left smooth. I’m not crazy about having a bow tied at the side with this long cord, so probably will either tuck the excess cord under the waist panel or into the waistband.I followed the size guide and ordered my typical size, XL, and they fit perfectly. When first opening the package, I was concerned that they may be too long. I have a 32” inseam, and these will be a perfect length with the 1” heels I plan to wear. With sneakers, these will be a shade long, but acceptable. For folks with shorter legs, these probably will be too long.These will be very nice for travel since they are very comfortable and can be packed with NO wrinkles! Under $15? Five Stars!!I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

Cute strings For some reason the strings in these yoga pants make it stand out from other yoga pants I like that it’s true to size and doesn’t fold back up They are a bit long for my 5’1 height so made for taller folks unless you wear wedges Good quality, breathable, stretchy, not too thin or thick

Love the pockets! Initially I got these to wear as pajama pants now that the weather is getting colder. I try to buy cotton as much as possible because for me it's more comfortable and these are 95% cotton 5% spandex which is perfect because the spandex helps them keep their shape. If they were 100% cotton they would most likely get baggy and shapeless over time without the spandex, so thats a plus. Anyway, when I tried them on ( I am 5'2", 155lbs, usually a med/large in bottoms ) I was very happy with the fit. I ordered a large according to the size chart because since they're cotton I will have a little room if they shrink a bit. I carry most of my weight up top and have 41" hips and a fairly flat booty. So they fit fine with a little room to spare, I am hoping they will shrink a tiny bit but its so close I'll be happy either way. As far as quality I am impressed. The material is thicker than a high quality T-Shirt but not as thick as your average sweat pants. For me thats perfect for fall and spring weather. It has a ribbed texture that gives it some charm. the waist band is not to wide with excellent stiching and a nice elastic but not to tight. The ties are functional and can be used to tighten the waist if needed or just for looks if not. The seams are well done, straight and even. The hems are good, equal size and well stiched. Last but not least the pockets are deep enough to carry a phone or keys etc. with no worries about falling out. I might even wear these for a grocery run or my grandsons soccer game. I would reccomend these to a friend.

Like them, but not as long as the look Pros - Nice ribbed materiel. Comfortable to sleep in. Nicer looking than most pajama pants, I feel like I could leave my house in these to get mail or something and be fine. Good drawstring Cons - I keep looking for PJs that will be long enough for me (5'9 woman). These are not them, but I'll keep looking. Shrunk a little in the wash too. The stitching can be kind of wonky in areas. Also, I washed them once and one of the pockets has a long hole in them now. Overall, I don't mind them, and for pajamas, they do just fine. Don't think I'd recommend them, however.

Love, Love, Love!!! These pants are so cute and comfortable that I immediately ordered another pair in a different color! I am 5’5” 185lbs and ordered the XL, and the fit was perfect! The fabric was thicker than I expected, which I was really happy about. I felt totally comfortable wearing these outside the house. These pants would be wonderful to travel in! I love the loose, comfortable fit, along with draw string waist, and pockets! If I find something I like, I want to share my experience, so others can buy with confidence. Great price on a quality product! These are a 10 out of 10 in my book!

These ribbed striped pants are so comfortable! I wish they had solid colors as well. I’m 5’9 and 123 lbs and ordered a medium. I like when things are loose compared to tight. Also the length I needed to be long enough. They fit perfectly and I washed on cold and hung to dry. The blue is a brighter electric blue but very cute and these have pockets! I would purchase again and hope for solid colors in the future

Shaped like Pajama Pants I love the idea of these pants, but unfortunately, they just don’t fit me very well. I think if I had sized up, they may have fit more comfortably. The waist was very stretchy and the legs were cut wide leg. I have thicker thighs and a big butt and these were a bit snug in both of those regions. There wasn’t any stretch so no extra give to accommodate. I thought that the wide legs would accommodate my bigger back side but that area wasn’t cut for a bigger backside. The material was lightweight and would have been nice for the spring or summer with a lightweight top. The color is a lovely light green stripe and due to it being so light would match with many different options for a top. The pants are a bit on the see through side as well. The length of the pants was a little long on my 5”5’ frame but would be perfect for many others. I wore the pants with some platform type clogs and it made them fit me much better.

Fit well. Material is thin, summer weight. I got the X-Large in black. I guess because of the stripes it makes it more of a dark gray color, to me. That is okay with me. I've included a photo that is sort of a close up of what the color and material looks like. The material surprised me with how thin it is though. Great summer weight pants due to the thinness. It was pretty warm (upper 70's) when I tried them on and they felt cool and soft on my skin. A plus for summer! I would not want to wear them in the winter without some thermals underneath as I do live in a state where it can get pretty cold in the winter. The fit is good. The only stretch they have in them is the waist band. The material has no stretch at all. With so many items of clothing having stretch to some degree in them, it is rare to find one that doesn't have it. The length is not too extra long and I will be able to wear them with a shoe that has a slightly thicker sole or even a low heel will be just fine. Being 5' 4" I often run into things being too long in the leg by the time I get it to fit my apple shaped torso. The legs are not as flowy on the thigh part as I thought they would be, but from the knees down, they're flowing nicely, lol. They look to be of decent quality. I did find one dangling thread along a seam on the lower leg, but a quick snip fixed that. Did not see any negative affects from getting rid of that. I will recommend these. Especially for summer or warmer climates. They do look very nice and can be dressed up or down, whatever the mood and occasion is. I did try these on a second time while writing this review and found that I liked them better than when I had tried them on earlier in the day. I am pleased with these pants and the options I have for wearing them.

They are thick without being too bulky! The sweatpants from the Trendy Queen store are thick and warm, great to use in the winter cold days. I got size X-Large, as I usually wear, and they fit well. For reference, my weight is 225 pounds, and my height is 5 feet and 10 inches. The material is 80% polyester and 20% cotton. The recommendation is to hand wash cold, but I do not have time for hand washing, so I washed them in the washing machine, in cold water, normal cycle, followed by drying. They came out great, and I did not notice any damage or shrinkage. Overall, I am pleased with these pants and I would recommend them to a friend!

SWEATPANTS... BUT FASHIONABLE! I ordered these Trendy Queen Wide Leg Sweatpants in Gray in a size medium. I looked at the size chart and decided to size down because I am on a weight loss journey. For reference, in this photo I am 5'5ish, 165ish with a 29" waist. These pants fit well! But, they will definitely look more how I want them to when I lose another 10 pounds. These sweatpants are truly comfortable! They are the usual fleece lined, Russel-style sweatpants that you can find at the big box store. The criss cross waistline is visually interesting and stretchy. The pleat down the front of the wide leg makes them appear more elevated than your usual ol' slouchy sweatpants. While I do wish that they were a couple of inches longer-- but I can totally deal with it. I plan on wearing these sweatpants with a black high neck cropped tank and athletic shoes. I wore them out to run errands wearing this today... even my doctor commented on how cozy and cute they were! Comfortable Pants These are more like track or jogger pants than sweatpants. They don’t have the fleece lining that you find in heavy weight sweats. These are great for fall or spring though. Surprisingly heavy I never thought I would use the word "heavy" to describe sweatpants, but that's exactly what these are. Taking them out of the package my first thought was that they feel heavier than a pair of jeans. I was curious so I actually weighed them and they are 1.4 lbs. That may not seem like much, but I also weighed a pair of normal lounge pants and they were under 0.5lb. That means these pants are almost 3 times heavier than normal lounge pants. The material is thick (obviously) but somehow they aren't warm at all and are actually cool to the touch. The waist fit ok, but the legs were gigantic and very boxy. The length was good, but I prefer my pants on the longer side. For reference, I'm 5'4 ad 135lb and ordered size medium. The material seems like decent quality, but I just didn't like how they felt while wearing them.

CAUTION: GIVES OUT COLOR DURING WASHES. MUST BE WASHED SEPARATELY This product Arrived on time and in perfect condition! No damages at all. ✓ Fit - I am 5'2" - 115 lbs - US Size 2. The fit was perfect at the waist and the length comes exactly up to my ankles when the pants are fully stretched. Sometimes when I stand up after sitting for a while, these pants tend to behave like Capri pants and I have to stretch them again. This isn't much of an issue for me. But anyone taller than me must note that they can use these pants only as Capri pants and not as full length harem pants. ✓ Material - Quilt type. ✓ Quality - Confusing. The fabric as such is really good. Not easily transparent. Cotton. Perfect vacation wear. But it does give out a lot of color. During the first wash, I accidentally put these pants on the washer along with a White top and as a result I've got a candy pink top now ( kinda like Rachel in FRIENDS). I've attached a picture of that top for your reference. Also, this pant keeps on giving out color for the first many washes. I'm not sure when this is gonna stop. I've made it a point to always wash this separately. I've attached a picture of the color of water during my second time washing this pant. You can see that it is still pretty much dark. ✓So now that brings me to my next point. The fabric color tends to fade after a few washes. First when I wore these pants, they were a nice dark shade of red. But now, the color isn't so ravishing red anymore. After a few washes, the color has faded drastically ( picture attached). Also, the pants shrink a little after the first couple of washes. ✓ So then why have I given this product 4 Stars? It is because of the fact that these pants are extremely comfortable. I bought this before going on a trip to India. These pants were so comfortable that they were by default my go to pants during my entire trip (Cotton feels really good in tropics and sub tropics!). These pants can be paired with a black or white crop top/ tank top or even a longer top with 3/4th sleeve. Looked good always. I even wore this during my flight back. Now that I'm back, I still wear these pants a lot. Got a lot of compliments. ✓ The elastics are of good quality too. The tummy elastic doesn't make you look puffed up at all. On the contrary it actually helped me a little in tummy control. ✓ Cannot be worn as a jumpsuit. ***Update: Have reduced my rating to 3 stars ✓ It has been more than 6 months since I purchased this product. These pants still bleed color. I've washed it separately many times now (lost count). ✓ Everytime these pants have to be washed, it must be done so separately. I'm having to hand wash these pants. It takes up a lot of my time and energy ( Not to mention the fact that it takes up a lot of water). ✓ Color has faded a lot. Forget Ravishing Red. It isn't even red anymore. Rather a deep orange. ✓ I'm also having to air dry these a lot since the dryer shrinks them. ✓ So in conclusion, I'd like to say that this Product would require a lot of maintenance and honestly isn't worth it. PS. I've attached a picture taken during the last time I washed these pants. Please do note the color of the water!

Beautiful pants but not impressed with service from vendor I will update this review if I hear back from Tribe Azure. I have ordered these pants twice now. The first time the pants got lost in the mail and after weeks waiting for them I got a refund from Amazon. So disappointing. The second time I ordered them I received a tiny package with the pants scrunched into a ball, wrapped in cheap saran wrap. Not a great initial impression. But them I took them out of the wrap and although the pants are beautiful, they are sized a small/medium. I ordered a large for my 5'9", 140 lbs frame. After trying them on it's apparent they might work as rather bulky shorts., only if I wear the smocked part over my entire lower frame. I was hoping for full length wide harem pants as the photos advertising on Amazon show. Again, disappointing. These would only fit someone size 0 - 4 as their size chart says. I sent an email through Amazon to Tribe Azure and am awaiting a reply.

👠 Light Weight, Smooth & Cool Fabric with a 27.4” inseam For Reference I am ( 36 C *** 5’4” *** 162 lbs ) I received the size Large in the Taupe color . The color is neutral and sides a bit on the tan spectrum. The Fabric is comprised of 75% Nylon and 25% Spandex so it is very stretchy and flexible, which makes these joggers perfect for working out, yoga or in my case they performed very well at pilates class. The versatility of these joggers makes them perfect for lounging or for casual wear also. I paired them with a flannel shirt and some platform uggs for a quick trip to the market. The smoothness of the material offers comfortability, while the coolness of the fabric helps makes them perfect for activities and various seasons, tho I would not be wearing these during the winter months. I like that they are light weight , but unfortunately not so breathable. The Inseam on the main listing page is stated to be 25” which is what drew me to them , except they are also listed on the lower description portion as 27.4” . I decided to measure them and they come in at 27.4” inseam, making them a bit long on me for my height. A great feature of these joggers is the waistband which is not too wide or high and fits true to size. It offers a working drawstring, 2 generously sided pockets and a cuffed hem They would make a comfy travel companion and pack easily but do have the tendency to gather at the crotch area. They are machine washable in cold cycle only, they will shrink in warm to hot water. There are an abundance of different neutral matte colors offered, the brown being another nice option for me, especially if they offer a petite inseam.

I'm obsessed - maybe size down? Wow! I have a lot of joggers, including several higher-end pairs like Vuori, but I reach for these in my drawer! The fabric is incredible; it's hard to describe: sort of silky (not t-shirt material like Vuori), super stretchy, and thin but somehow not in a cheap way. It's sort of like the fabric used for Lulu Align leggings but in jogger form. I will say that the taupe color washes me out, but that's a ME problem, not an issue with the joggers. The stitching is excellent, the pockets are plenty deep enough for a phone, and the waistband is super generous and comfortable with a true elastic drawstring. If you are between sizes, I would size DOWN. I am 5'6" and 130-140 with an athletic build (read: built like a rectangle with zero waist) so I usually have to size up to not be squished in the waist, but these are almost too loose on me. I got a medium and definitely could have gotten away with a small, but this larger size just has a nice, comfy lounge vibe, and that's fine, too. The ankles are tapered but again, a bit loose on me. Vuori brags that you can work out in their joggers but I actually WOULD work out in *these* joggers, and I could also sleep in them, travel in them (perfect airplane pants!), and feel put together enough to run errands in them. Given the quality, this is an amaaaaazing price for these. I'll be ordering more!

user_comment: Exactly what I was wanting except 1 pair Love them. Exactly what I was looking for. I think these fit as expected. If you want them real baggy order a size up. Otherwise order your regular size. For reference I'm 130# 5'6" I ordered small. Waist for jeans 25" Edit: so I ordered 4 pairs of these sweats. One pair I do not have yet but of the three that I have received, my above comment applies to two of the pairs. The light blue once in the picture and the maroon ones with white lettering Unfortunately we're not able to rate each pair individually and I don't think it's fair to down rate based on one bad pair. Overall I'm happy with my order. However I am warning you guys to beware of the pinkish ones with white lettering. You can tell right away when clicking on the different colors that this one was pictured differently. This one I feel pretty safe to say it's not legit. The tags and everything appear to be but you can tell immediately that the lettering down the left leg is different than the other pairs. It's clearly an iron on. It's smooth surface and the edges are going to peel. Where as my other pairs are more textured and seem to be an actual part of the product. These also were made in a different country than the other ones. Also the color is very misrepresenting. It's more of a pumpkin or a dull Coral coloring. Unfortunately these were the most expensive out of the four pairs I ordered and my gut was telling me something wasn't right with these just off the picture but based off the reviews I also thought it would be a good little research project to order multiple colors and see what happened. The reviews seem to one extreme or the other. I've added pictures of these as well. Simply for the color to be noted

Poor Quality… I bought them to wear after swimming bc they’re lightweight, loose fitting, and inexpensive. I’m a size 12/14, hourglass shape (smaller waist, wide hips, bubble butt). The XL fits well. I’m 5’6 and they are the perfect length. The cuff hits right above my foot. I washed them on delicate before wearing, but didn’t air dry like the tag says. After 1 wash, the printing cracked and a small amount was coming off (Pictures #1-3). Not great, but I don’t use them for much. After 3 wears, the fabric is pilling inside the thighs (Picture #4). They’re only used for going to and from the pool, not for everyday wear. It’s not a big deal if the print peeled a bit, but the pilling after 3 wears tells me these wouldn’t hold up for more strenuous use. The pants look more orange in some of my pictures, but they’re actually light pink like the product photos show. The laundering recommendations on the tag say to hand wash in cold water and line dry.

Comfortable, quality pants Sometimes size guides can be tricky. I'm 5'5, 118 lbs, and decided to go with an XS. I don't like baggy pants in general, even with sweatpants. These fit well for me, and I do have decent size hips, and they still fit fine; not tight, and not baggy. My inseam is short (about 27"),so I normally purchase pants from the juniors department. These pants are a pinch long, but the closed bottom hem does a good job of preventing the material from spilling onto the floor while I walk, without being too tight at the bottom either. I use these mainly as lounge pants and they have washed well. The pockets fit my Samsung Note 10+, although it does slightly hang out the side at the top, but keys, slim wallets, etc fit deep inside without a problem. I bought these in a few colors and I think the price makes it a good deal. Edit: I bought the Blue Yonder Heather one and although the size label was what I ordered, the fit was way off.. closer to a medium or large, when I ordered a XS. The print was different too. The other colors I ordered were fine though. Second edit: Have bought a few more of these, most recently the royal blue color. I personally think these pants run big as I ordered the XS and the size chart says I should get a small. Wanted to add that my hips are 38" and my waist is 27". I currently have the Samsung z fold 3 and it fits fine in the pocket when the screen is folded. I recommend.

My problem, as you can see in the picture, is that the logo along the leg is peeling off in little flakes, and now it just looks horrible. I don’t find it necessary to wear labels and I wish that I could just entirely remove it, but that’s not happening, so I’m stuck with it as I watch it deteriorate and look worse and worse. Aside from that, it leaves little blue flakes on the other clothes that I wash it with now. Also mention that I wash only in cold water on a normal or gentle cycle, which is more reason why I think that this should definitely not be happening, I might understand it more if I used hot water. So now I wear them when I don’t plan on seeing anybody, or on leaving the house for any reason at all. I really really don’t like how it looks, and this is the only reason I will never order these sweatpants again.

Pregnancy approved! So being pregnant and a tight skinned whale I hate all bottoms right now. Everything is uncomfortable to wear. I wanted to find a pair of pants like these that I could wear to the hospital and back home after giving birth! Or at-least for postpartum use if they were too small or tight on my tummy. My usual weight is in the 130s lbs but with baby number two I’m currently at 170 lbs at 8 months. I wasn’t sure what size to get so I just got my usual pre-pregnancy medium for the after birth. But surprisingly they fit my lower pregnant tummy just fine! No tightness or discomfort spandex pants give me. And they fit great under my tummy too so I know they’ll fit great once baby’s out. I’m just worried if my belly continues to grow these last 2 months it’ll stretch the waist elasticity so I’m not gonna wear it too much to preserve it’s youth lol. But definitely recommend for pregnant people! If you’re less weight or roundness than me medium should fit fine! Bigger than me I would say medium -large just so you don’t suffer from the compression if it gets too tight.

True petite and comfortable For reference, I'm 5"3, 28 waist, 35 hips, 118 lbs and got the brand's size 4 in black (since I'm normally size small) and they fit like a glove. These area are thin but not sheer. Keep in mind I got the color black and I also wear black underwear underneath. I can't speak for other color options. At least, the ones I got are not see through. I wore them to play pickup in relatively high humidity and sweat buckets. They stayed on pretty well and remained their integrity and comfortable the whole time. Sure I pulled them up and/or adjusted from time to time but that's normal for leggings. Even though black doesn't really show sweat marks, with how much I sweat and how wet the leggings got, I would certainly assume other lighter color options would show very visible sweat marks. If you don't wear them to do things that will cause you to sweat like crazy, other colors would be fine. (or if you don't care much about visible sweat marks on your leggings) Because these are petite, they're actually 7/8 length on me. Yay. Fabric is soft, stretchy, and comfortable. They stretch quite a bit with no bad squeeze. I would not call these compression leggings, and depend on your body build, they may or may not offer tummy control. Compared to my other leggings, though, I personally wouldn't call these tummy control or would not get these for their tummy control feature. But that's me. One thing I noted during my high intensity pickup was I heard a slight, low ripping sound a couple of times when I adjusted the leggings. They did not rip but the sound was not reassuring. I can say I'm not rough with my leggings when I adjust them. Hopefully, these will last a good amount of time.

Really soft, comfortable and stretchy! I recently sized down from S/M to XS/S so gave it a try to the size 4 instead of my previous usual choice of 6 here. When I received the package, however, it became apparent I made a mistake sizing down. It looked too small and I gave up without actually trying it on. But then, after a week or two and my monthly phases pass on, I just felt like, why don't I give it a, actual try-on before giving it away? So I did. I am super glad I did! It fits perfectly! Despite the look being quite small as-is, it turned out to be really really soft and stretchy and yet not see-through! I am 5'3"/110lbs and size 4 was perfect for me here, while I think 6 might still be a good fit for me for the other half of a month. The fabric, the texture and touch, overall cuts are just 🤌 perfect! Also, the color is very vibrant and beautiful a shade of green (my phone didn't do justice though). Highly recommend to give it a try!

Nice pants! Lovely comfortable pants! Great for warm weather! I wore these nice pants in the color Black in 86F weather for a 9 mile hike and these were comfortable throughout allowing for full range of motion! These feel fairly breathable and the fabric is cool on the skin. They also don't show sweat. Love the zipper pockets! These are amazing for having zippers in both the front pockets and the back pocket, there is also velcro on the side pockets! All the pockets are deep and the front pockets and side pockets are deep enough to hold my smartphone! The front pockets are especially roomy! I ordered the size Medium in Charcoal/Black combo and am 5'1" 113 lbs. 33-25-37 dimensions and each thigh is 20". These fit really nicely but due to my shorter height these fit more like long pants. I think if you have similar dimension you could possibly order a size Small for a more fitted look. I personally like the looseness as it's very comfortable and breezy! The colors of the pants seem accurate to the pictures. The charcoal is a medium-dark grey. Like these activewear pants and they feel durable, the fabric has nice quality and like the pants will last many washings! 5/5!

Great price and quality This 3 pack PJ bottoms are great. Lightweight, stretchy, thin, and breathable material. Great quality. Comfortable to sleep in or lounging in. Can’t beat the price. I ordered size small and fits great, I’m 5’3” and weigh 130 lbs for size reference. I also like that each one is tagged separately so you can gift them individually to family or friends. Perfect for summer, but can be worn all year. I will definitely buy again and highly recommend.

OBSESSED Love this sweatpants set!!! I got it in the black-lilac-gray color and size M. I’m a size 6 or 28/29 in bottoms and they fit perfectly on me, super comfy and doesn’t pinch or squeeze in the waist at all. If I wanted it more form fitting I could have gone down a size but I like my sweatpants comfy. I’m also 5’3 and usually have to get a short inseam, and these go all the way to the floor on me but aren’t too long and don’t bunch at the bottom. EDIT: I’m taking off 1 star because after wearing them for a few weeks I noticed a bunch of loose threads and a bit of pilling in between the thighs. However I still wear them all the time and after some washes they’re still super soft. Pros: - great value!!! Such a good price for 3 sweatpants - amazing quality and fabric. Sooo soft, thick, feels like premium sweatpants from a high end brand but much much cheaper. Could def wear in winter - great colors. The lilac is cute but not too bright, a nice muted pastel pink/purple The only thing to note is that each color fits slightly different, but they all fit great on me in general. The black is the roomiest and the lilac is slightly lower waisted than the others (see pics). Very very slight differences though. Would highly recommend!!!

At first I immediately thought these pants were going to be too long. I’m 4’11” however with my Clark’s booties and course wearing this pants high waisted as they are designed that way, the pants fell exactly were they should, top of the shoe and no material dragged behind. I was so surprised to have them fit and feel amazing. The color, which I tried best to show their exact color of green in the picture is a rich dark green. At first my husband thought they were black but in more lite areas of the home and of course a work environment or natural lighting, they are obviously green. According to my personalized color stylists, I was recommended to steer clear of black because I’m a true summer palette and a way of doing that when wanting to dress up is wearing dark green and navy as to why I chose these pants. For $17 (holiday deal/overstock) these are excellent quality material, they almost feel like yoga pants yet look so professional. The waist is very comfortable because of the thick elastic waistband. This color of green could go with black, brown, silver, gold. Again for reference, because I know when it’s a struggle reading reviews trying to decide, I’m 4’11”, 115lbs, rectangle shape, and ordered small green.

The fabric is 70% viscose, 25% nylon and 5% spandex with instructions to wash in medium temperature water and use medium iron for touch up. With the fabric being primarily viscose which is a form of rayon, I think I’ll dry mine on rack inside since rayon is noted for losing its shape if not handled carefully when wet.

Updated Review: I absolutely loved these pants when I got them but the second time wearing them, the elastic around the waste started coming apart (see pic) and once it starts, it ALL unravels. I tried a couple of things to try to get it the elastic to stop unraveling but no such luck. So I've lost two of the eight elastics bands on the waist and the waist wasn't that tight to start with so if another elastic goes, they will be falling off me. Hoping that doesn't happen though (and I had already ordered a second pair that I will have to return now since these are not cheap pants at $30/pair) Original Review: These are WAY better than I was expecting and I plan on buying another pair now. I like pants like this on hot summer days - I find because they are loose and flowy that they are not as hot as wearing capris for example. They are a super soft fabric and long (I'm 5'9) and they have pockets which I didn't realize, but luckily the pockets don't pucker or puff out so you can't even tell there are pockets which is how I like my pockets :) When I first saw them, I thought the waist may be too small but the elastic is soo stretchy that they easily fit and don't create a "muffin" top or anything like that which is awesome!!

Not Squat Proof I really really wanted to love these, but they just aren't what I need. First of all, they are definitely more of a capri than the picture shows. I thought they were closer to a 7/8 but they hit just below my knees (and I'm short). Second, I bought the blue and I thought it was going to be more of a brighter blue based on the picture and it was closer to a navy. And last, it didn't pass the squat test. Even with being a darker color, I could still see the undies clear as day. In the picture I'm just stretching them a little with my hands, and you can see just how much light is getting through. If you aren't doing a major workout these may be okay. They were comfortable. I am a soft 136 lbs, 5'3", and usually an 8 in clothing and I got the medium. I possibly could have gotten the large, but guarantee I could not have gone smaller. I really was close to keeping these, as they were close to being good, but I took many pictures in them from different angles and in natural light and I just couldn't feel good about them.

Sizing Don’t Listen To Them! I was back and forth with seller about the appropriate size for me, I kept changing from L to XL, both were inaccurate reviews and seller. I know because I used my own common scene and ordered the size I knew would fit at my size. 190lbs and went with the XL and the fit was perfect. The seller indicated I get a size 3X….REALLY…never in my entire life wore nothing higher than a XL. The reviews are insane as we’ll. All I would say if you want a looser fit, size up, other then that use my guide, it’s more then most. They are stretch and if your over 200 lbs don’t buy no XL you will be sending them back for a larger size. 170-180lbs Large. Shipping was guaranteed sooner too, that didn’t happen, wasn’t too long though. Very nice pair of jeans overall. Listen to your body, if you need to squeeze into any pair of jeans then that’s on you.

MY FAVORITE PANTS I LOVE THESE! I'm 5'2" so buying pants online is always a little bit of a chance because usually they're too long but these are perfect! I'm also curvy so I usually don't wear straight leg "mom jean" type pants because they can make me look frumpy, but these are super flattering! I got them in September 2023 (6 months ago) and they are still in perfect condition, I've worn them ice skating, sledding in the snow and they're still in perfect condition. They're not very thick and feel thin so I was worried they'd rip when I totally failed at ice skating but they didn't. Because they're thin they don't get too hot or feel too thick, and I wore leggings under them in the snow and they worked great and I didn't get cold. They also have a lining on the inside so it feels like I'm wearing swishy pants. I wear them at work a lot with a blouse so they work for a business setting, and they're my go to coffee shop pants so they can be dressed down and up. I'm literally going to wear these until they die then buy another pair.

I sized up and they fit comfortably, very soft fabric, the color doesn't quite match the pics though I'm 5'5"/120 lbs and usually a medium but I got a large as I like to size up for loungewear to ensure it's comfortable. I'm pleased with the way these fit. They are loose enough to be comfy but they still look nice and the waist isn't tight at all though it still stays up on its own. They would probably be too long for me if it wasn't for how they're made to bunch up at the ankles but as is, they are fine. The material is very soft and it's lightweight enough to be nice for hotter weather but not so thin as to be see-through or anything. They do advise hanging these to dry so I wash them on delicate and hang them up. The pockets are nice and roomy but you can see the outline of them while you wear them because of that as it bunches up. There are a couple loose stitches here and there but they seem pretty nicely made overall. The elastic seems sturdy for the waistband and is pretty thick. The drawstring is the only part that seems a little cheap. It's knotted a couple inches down kind of haphazardly and it's very thin and looks a little off. My other small complaint is that the color isn't quite what I expected. In the pictures, they look like a deep purple but to me they look more like a navy blue in person which was a little disappointing but in general I'm happy with this purchase.

Cute sizing is strange So they are super cute, comfy, and the colors are amazing. I do wear them regularly but they are definitely made for a certain body type. They aren't jean material like I thought they would be, they are stretchy yoga pant material. With that being said there are no pockets or belt loops. Let me start with telling y'all I'm 5ft 10inches and 142 pounds and ended up with the medium. If you are tall and thin like me you may run into some issues with sizing. The leg length to waist ratio wasn't too great. If I got the correct size in the waist (small) I needed 2 sizes up for length. But the size that fit my length (large) are huge in the waist. So I went in the middle and bought a medium. They are a little shorter than I would like and a little baggy in the waist. Since there is no belt loops there is not much I can do with that. So for us tall ladies there is definitely an issue in sizing.

My new yoga pants I absolutely love these!! They're very soft, stretchy, and not see through. I'm 5'10 and wear an American Eagle 12 Long but these pants are just a tad too long when I wear Vans. So I'd keep that in mind if you don't have long legs. The only other thing I'd note about these is that they have NO belly control. I take comfort in having even a little (even though I have a flat stomach) but if you have a belly or are just self conscious, I'd be a little weary. I'd also note that if you don't have hips, these pants may be difficult to stay on you. I get the impression that these slide down on others because they slide a little on me. Just got reference: I'm 5'10, 42 inch hips, 32 inch waist. American Eagle pant size 12 Long.

Certain Colors Are Made Differently I purchased two colors to see if either would work for a 70s party. Although the measurements and fabric on both pair are listed as being identical, the measurements and fabric were actually different. I’m 5’10 barefoot, weigh 185 lbs., and ordered a Large. Black Pair: The black pair were 2-3 inches shorter than the printed black-and-blue ones. They were highwaters on me without wearing shoes. The fabric was extremely thin, which is great for summer. They were also comfortable and stretchy, but they’re made like a super-thin, cheap t-shirt…or the cheapest black leggings you can find. A medium would’ve been fine, but I ordered Larges in hopes of having extra length. The black pair will pick up lint (etc.) fairly easy, they wrinkle fast and came out of the package SUPER wrinkled, and they’ll surely fade or shrink after a wash or two. Great purchase for the price if you only want a few wears out of them. Black-and-Blue Pair: The quality was much better. The black-and-blue pair had a little more “suction” and elasticity, they were just as comfortable, and they were much longer. I can wear them with heels and they’ll be long enough. The picture shows how they looked right out of the package, which is very different from the black pair. Hope this helps!

As expected. 44 Waist, 50" Hips, 5' 7", 235lbs. I ordered 6 pair on a lightening deal, two of the colors are t at all what I expected, the orange is more like a pumpkin rather than a Halloween orange, and the light blue is far more periwinkle, purple/blue. I don't care for either color but for the price it's whatever. They seem to be identical in fabric to Leg Depot leggings. Same 8% spandex. 3x-5x at LD is much bigger though, too big actually for me. I already checked the fabric content against LD so knew they would pretty be the same minus the fun prints.

Nice white stretchy pants I’m not sure about these pants. I like the bright whiteness of them and I like that there’s pockets. They’re definitely a yoga style pant however, I feel like you could wear them as leggings if you want to. My issue with them is that they’re not as thick as I would of hoped to have had them, now they are not see-through however, because they’re not that thick they don’t do everything you want, leggings or yoga pants to do. Hide “things”! It’s hard to tell in these pictures with great lighting(I am using a ring light), but they’re not hiding everything you would want to hide. Ss a matter of fact, in some bad lighting, or maybe even a little bit of shadowing, it kind of makes your legs look even worse than what they probably are. So if you have dimples or any kind of cottage cheese going on in the thigh/hamstring/glutes area, you’re not gonna wanna wear these by themselves, ladies you know what I mean. But I feel they would be a good layering pant. Or perfect for a young lady that has nice smooth legs or super fit no jiggle or sagging in the skin yet hahah! They are definitely stretchy, so that’s a plus. I am 5‘9“ 145 to 150 pounds and these are a size medium.

Cute and smooth These are cute but not what I was expecting, my fault for not noticing the word “shaping”. I wanted plain, smooth, stretchy leggings with no compression or shaping that are also totally flat under dresses and tops so they don’t show through. I like these because of the smooth material and how high they sit and fit at the top. They would be perfect if they weren’t shaping. I wanted something to lay around in but can’t seem to find simple plain totally smooth stretchy capri leggings. My old pair I got from Kohl’s a decade ago finally ripped and can’t find anything to replace them. These are pretty close but not as easy to relax in. I hate shape wear, but really in the L/XL it isn’t too bad. The S/M were miserably tight so I had to order up. I am 5”4 132 lbs but prefer the L/XL, they still shape and fit well without making my guts feel squished and horrible. They are still a bit too tight in the crotch and legs compared to what I am used to but again not terrible just not what I was looking for. They are made of good material for the price, and great if you’re looking for shape wear. They are helping flatten my postpartum belly pretty well so that is a plus.

Comfortable pants! I just tried these on and they are so soft and comfortable. I was looking for lounge pants for home & travel that were not tight or too baggy and these are perfect. Love these! I loved these pants when I saw the picture because they reminded me of my favorite cargo scrub pants. Those were the most comfortable pants, so I had high hopes. I was a little worried when I saw pants in S, M, L, etc., but I hoped that meant there was some leeway in sizing. I wasn't disappointed! I'm 5' 3" and 142 lbs and I bought the MEDIUM. I have long legs for a short woman. I didn't measure the inseam in these, but I'm going to guess 32" or 33". I left my shoes off in the photo, so you could see where the length hit me. I normally wear 32" or 34" inseam depending on the kind of shoes I'm wearing, so these are exactly as long as I was hoping. These snow pants are high waisted, which means no snow falling down the back of your pants. The fit of these is flattering, but they are roomy enough that I could wear another pair of pants underneath. In the photos, I'm just wearing the pants and you can see where I have some room, without there being too much room. I'm looking forward to wearing my favorite Cuddle Duds leggings under these when we get super cold. Our temps are often in the teens during winter with some negative temps sprinkled in. The back waist is elastic and it also has belt loops. The pants have 2 zippered side pockets (one on each side), one zippered back pocket, and one zippered leg pocket. The back and leg pocket are both on the right side. All pockets can easily be zipped and unzipped with one hand. The zipper pulls have an easy-grip fob, so there's no fumbling to get into pockets, even with gloves. Additionally, all pockets are big enough to hold my iPhone XR without any magic tricks, so these pockets are generous. When I read these pants were water resistant, I worried they might make a swishing sound when I walk — like some track pants do — but they don't. They are totally quiet when I walk. The pants have a smooth satiny feel. They are soft, inside and out. The inside of the pants are not fluffy and bulky — think of the lining as being sueded and you'll have the best idea of what the warm lining is like. The fabric is thick without being bulky. I live in a really cold climate and I've been wearing these whenever I leave the house. I've comfortably worn them down to 10 degrees, so far. Normally my legs are my weakest area in my cold weather gear, but these pants have changed that since they are very warm and I can wear leggings (or even regular pants) under them. These snow pants are super comfortable — even for wear around the house. They zip up and snap. I love the snap and it has a little velcro tab to help keep things smooth and together. They're well made and look really nice. You can't tell they are snow pants. The just look like cargo scrubs or tactical pants. They have these cool seams in the knees (like my favorite cargo scrub pants did) that make it so you can bend down without the pants binding in the knees. I don't have a single complaint about these pants! They've upped my winter game. I'd love it if they had colors like dark plum purple and wine. I'd definitely purchase at least one more pair if they had colors like that, but I may even be back for another pair of the black ones, so I can wear a pair while the other is in the wash. I know some people are very concerned about fabrics with odors, but I didn't notice any odor at all when I took these out of the package. I've washed them and they came out just like they went in, but softer. All in all, these are great pants and I'm happy I found them!

Great quality and true to size! I am a security account manager and work in a cold warehouse. We have a uniform that is issued, but they do not provide warm pants. Before buying these pants I had to wear leggins under my uniform pants just to keep warm, but it was uncomfortable. I am 5'6" and weigh 135 so I ordered a medium. They look professional, are well made and keep me warm without the extra layer of leggins! These pants fit well and are comfortable to wear, the length is long enough to go over my boots and the zip pockets give extra protection from loosing anything you may have in them. I will be buying another pair to get me through Michigans cold winter!

Quiet & comfortable I am 5'8", a standard size 16 with a 31/32" inseam. I carry my weight a little bit in my belly, but am proportionally filled out throughout the bum and thigh. The XXL was a smidge large- but not enough for me to want to size down - to allow for base layers if needed. I would guess these would be a 16/18.Pros: Quiet- very important to me that they didn't sound like snowpants. Soft fleece interior with satiny pockets. The closure has both a snap and hook-n-loop tab and there are belt loops well placed around the waist. These are what I would consider mid to high waisted and didn't feel like I would expose my crack when bending over.Cons: Pockets! Can womens pants please start having the same size pockets as mens! There is only one back pocket and the thigh "cargo" pocket is just a straight-flat pocket ( the pleat is just for looks - I'm going to remove the one seam to allow it to be a real cargo pocket) AND both of these pockets are on the right side so if you have stuff in both, your pants will be very heavy on the right side. Also, the front comes up a little high for my tastes and is a little floofy in the pooch area. I feel they could drop the front at least an inch and they would almost fit perfectly.I've now tried 6 different, highly rated pairs of fleece lined pants - even high-end name brand pairs and these are the ones I'll be using on my upcoming hike. For the price, they seem to be of good quality and have been the most comfortable so far. If anything changes after the hike, I will update my review.

Cozy but a bit odd I’m 5’1” and weigh 183 lb. I got the 2x which at first felt a bit tight in the crotch and thighs but seemed to loosen up within an hour or so of wearing. I think I’ll be able to wear a thin base layer underneath. The pants are comfortable and the lining feels very cozy though the cut seems a little odd to me. The crotch is higher in the front than the back and there is some puckering along parts of the seam. I can’t speak to how weatherproof they are, but hoping for the best. The outer material is soft yet substantial. I like that the waist is elastic in the back with a zip front. The pockets are a good size.

Comfortable and good fit I am 5'9", 140 lbs. 29" waist. These pants fit great and are very comfortable. Extra room around waist to tuck in a few layers. They are soft and have ease of movement. Another pair I tried were a stiffer fabric making it harder to bend my knees (TacVasen). Would like 1 inch longer but will work ok with boots. Going to Ireland in two weeks so these should work great with 40-50 degree rainy weather.

Stay cozy and dry in these light-weight dogwalkers/hiking/camping pants Thoughtful details like a deep side leg pocket to slide your phone in and out of. They have elastic around half the waist for a snug fit (but not tight). Side zip pockets. One Pocket in rear. A snap closer with some added velcro also. I am 5'9 and 147lbs and the large fits perfectly but I like them a bit loose in the back ( they are NOT form fitting). If you want a sleeker look- (well. These aren't the pants for you, if you want a sleeker look! They are cargo pants with soft , light fleece lining), I would go a size down. Otherwise I usually wear a size 8 or 10. Mostly a 10. And the large fits perfectly.

BEST WINTER HIKING PANTS EVER! I ordered these in two sizes to ensure I had a pair that would fit for First Day Hike. The second picture is me in size XL, which were way too big and long for me. Great pants for cold outdoor activities! I can’t recommend these “hiking” pants enough! I am 5’4” 140 lbs average build and height and I ordered Medium and they fit perfectly! I am a recreational hiker and snow shoer and these pants are very comfortable to move and climb in. Finally, they wash and dry very nicely and I find myself wearing these on cold days that I’m not even on the trail. I ordered grey and I’m definitely going to order another pair in black! Great value!

NOT windproof. I AM COLD! I work at an airport. I got these to combat the cold weather because I spend most of my day outside. I am sitting outside, waiting for a plane as I write this review. These pants are NOT windproof. They are very comfortable, but my legs are cold. Currently it’s overcast, windy, and 44 degrees F outside. I’m only keeping them because they fit better than my company-provided uniform pants.

good for taller ladies 1st my wife is 5 foot 2 inch 115 pounds and fairly athletic. Based on the sizing chart we got a size small pants. We found that the size was slightly baggy and longer compared to the photo. We wanted a hiking and rock climbing pants that she could use in cooler weather.PROS:They are very comfortable and extremely durable. This is a big plus for us since we do a lot of outdoor activities. My wife likes how these are warm but not too hot. She is very comfortable at 40 and 50 degree Weather wearing these pants. The fleece lining is very soft and not itchy to her. It has a strong elastic waist. The zippered pockets are snag free and are easy to grab with gloves on. The pants are water resistant and sheds water splashing well. I would believe they are water proof, but haven't had an opportunity to test or see it in action. The looser fit was a benefit because it gave her a big my flexibility and air circulation while being active. She was hoping for a bit more form fitting, but we will have to try the next size down. She is definitely happy with performance of these and wants to keep them.CONS:The fit is for a taller lady, by my guess someone in the 5' 4" or 5' 6" range for the size smalls and a few more pounds heavier. These are great for performance but not that flattering if your trying to achieve a sexier look in active wear. You can see around my wife's feet that there is a lot of extra material and room for a taller person.

The fleece lining is very soft and not itchy to her. It has a strong elastic waist. The zippered pockets are snag free and are easy to grab with gloves on. The pants are water resistant and sheds water splashing well. I would believe they are water proof, but haven't had an opportunity to test or see it in action. The looser fit was a benefit because it gave her a big my flexibility and air circulation while being active. She was hoping for a bit more form fitting, but we will have to try the next size down. She is definitely happy with performance of these and wants to keep them.CONS:The fit is for a taller lady, by my guess someone in the 5' 4" or 5' 6" range for the size smalls and a few more pounds heavier. These are great for performance but not that flattering if your trying to achieve a sexier look in active wear. You can see around my wife's feet that there is a lot of extra material and room for a taller person.

Well made, slim fit These have a nice fleece lining and are slim fitting so more flattering than a lot of snow pants. The leg is rather narrow so my Sorel snow boots would have be worn with the trousers tucked in. All my other boots and hiking boots will be fine. The zipper enclosures are well made and easy to open and I really liked the Velcro fastening. I would say they’re true to size but if in doubt or you’re between sizes then size up. For reference I’m 5’6, athletic build and 129lbs. I have enough room in these to wear leggings or Thermals underneath. There is some give in the waistband with it being elastic. They’re more flattering than the usual snow pants I’ve worn and will be easy to move around in if I’m hiking or shovelling snow.

Exactly what I wanted! These pants are exactly what I wanted for winter hiking and playing in the snow. They are incredibly comfortable and warm. The outside is water resistant. The water rolls right off. The inside is lined with fleece. I think that they look great on me. I was worried that they would be fluffy like traditional snow pants, they are not. They look and fit more like hiking cargo pants but they are durable and warm enough to wear as snow pants, great functionality. There are plenty of pockets and they are all zippered. That will be nice for sledding, I won't need to worry about my car keys getting lost in the snow. I am 5'6, 153 pounds and the large fits me perfectly. I am able to wear a thin pair of leggings under these pants. They're tight but it's do able.

Another pair going back Updated Review: I decided to try a size small. I am shocked they fit so much better I might just keep them. The small is the same length as the medium (weird) and they are still big enough for me to fit thermals underneath. The front of the pants pulls a little but it's not a total deal breaker for pants that I'll only wear in the outdoors.---- Original Review -----These pants felt nice and they seem like they would be warm. I am 5'4" tall and weigh 140 lbs. I am a little curvy and somewhat muscular. I have an hour glass figure and very little stomach poof. Also I have a low rise (high rise pants come up well over my navel). I like my outdoor pants to be baggy so I ordered a medium. The fit was just all kinds of wrong for my physique. As you can see from the photos the front of the pants are cut higher than the back. All of my women's pants are cut lower in the front than the back which leads me to think that these are guys pants with elastic in the back. The front of the pants are OK. The back of the pants are huge. Maybe I need a small but then I fear they won't be baggy. I think these are just short men's pants with elastic in the waist. If I were a guy these would fit so much better because then I'd have the stuff in the front to take up the space that makes the waist so high. I like my clothes to actually fit me properly and totally dislike when they don't. This is not something I sacrifice on. That being said, if you're OK with a not so great fit then these pants are nice and have deep pockets and just might be for you. Otherwise keep shopping.

These are ladies trousers so this review is by my partner: Lightweight fabric with a cosy fleece type lining. Plenty of zip pockets and a part elasticated waist for a better fit and comfort when moving around. I'm a size 16 and a pear shape do I tend to have difficulty finding trousers that aren't tight on my thighs and hips. The size XXL were a perfect fit with still room on my legs. The only issue is they are very long in the leg for my 5ft4 stature but I simply rolled the bottoms up. Good trousers and will buy more as this is the only pair of water resistant trousers I have which are great for walking and gardening in (as well as skiing!)

These trousers are absolutely fabulous. I've never had any fleece lined trousers before but my legs are always cold when I'm out so I wanted to try these. Now I want to change all my trousers to these. I got the black on size large. I'm size 14 and they fit me perfectly. The waist is 32" but the whole back waist strip is elastic so fits bigger sizes. The leg bottom is 7 1/2" and the inside leg length is 31". I'm 5'2" tall so they're a little long but I don't mind a bit extra at the bottom. They're fine once you put boots on anyway. The fabric is stretchy and really good quality. It's not shiny or crinkly fabric. The stitching all round is perfect. The pockets all have zips on them and they're good quality too. There are darts at the knees but not really obvious they just look like normal trousers. I can't believe how soft and warm they felt when I tried them on. They fit me perfectly and I literally can't find one thing wrong with them. I'm about to buy another pair as they're worth every penny in my opinion I love them. These are perfect if you work outside or to wear for outdoor events like Christmas markets or even indoors like me as I'm always cold. These are definitely a good buy and I don't think any buyer would be disappointed. 100% Recommended

I do a fair amount of very brisk walking. I got these pants as a lightweight option to protect me from the sun. Initially, I was put off by the material. Given they're 77% nylon, I was expecting more of a nylon feel. They are incredibly thin (quick-dry!) and have great stretch, not restrictive at all. The wide waistband sits comfortably, without slipping down. The pockets have hidden zippers, which is doubly beneficial. No worries about anything falling out or the zipper getting snagged on anything else. The only drawback, is my own fault. They're short on me. Somehow, I missed the 26.5" inseam in the product description. Unfortunately, once I get moving, the ankle cuffs ride up further. If I move the higher sitting waistband lower, I can get a better ankle cuff length, but then the pants look really saggy under my butt. They are quite comfortable. Though the length becomes an issue for a 15-20km walk, I will still use them for other shorter activities where the length won't be so persistently annoying.

Amazing pants, love the material and I was so excited that everything fit well.. except for the length. There’s two sizing charts that both have different inseam lengths than what I received. I’m 5’6 and got the XS, 31.9” would have been a perfect length but they came as 30” long which makes them look too short. I would size up for the length but sadly the waist would be too big.

Willit linen pants are the most comfortable linen pants I have so far. I was worried about the typical linen material being scratchy or itchy but they we were not. They are 55% Linen and 45% Cotton, perhaps because of this blend the material is soft to the skin. The material is also not thin yet breathable.The sizing is true to size and the style is wide leg and is flowy from the hips down. It’s also high waisted with an elastic waist band and a draw string. The inseam is 31inch and is a bit too long for me being 5’2”. Great function with two side pockets and two back pockets.Overall the linen pants are well made and sewn. As shown in my pictures, you can see the inside seam finish at the waist band has a wrapped lining and the pant leg hem finish is hidden.They defiantly added quality to their finish and for that, 5 stars.

These pants are very flattering and well made. They really show my figure in a good way. The pockets sit flat and they are roomy, and the overall construction is excellent. The fabric is pleasant and somehow warm, the pants are made of a linen and cotton blend. To note, there is absolutely no stretch in the fabric, aside of course from the elastic band at the waist. Fit wise, the pants fit as expected, maybe a little bit large. I take L or XL , I ordered Xl and they were fitting me perfect this morning. As an observation, the fabric is such that it gets a little bit larger and larger as the day goes by, something that both linen and cotton jeans tend to do most often. There is an adjustment tie at the waist which is most welcome nice the pants fit me large. The length is quite short on me, a few inches above the ankle. When I sit the length goes up to mid calf on me (I am 5’7”) The pants are very comfortable and ideal for spring and fall, I think they might be too warm for the summer. They look great with clogs or sandals, since they are too short to wear them with socks. Recommended! Thank you

According to their size chart, I wear a XXL, so that's what I ordered, and I was pleased to fit perfectly in the joggers. I'm also 5'10, and the length is perfect—not too short. The only thing is that the fabric leaves very little to the imagination. There's no cotton (80% nylon, 20% spandex), so it's super stretchy but very shiny, and it's not flattering. The fabric shows your underwear demarcation and everything else—cellulitis, you name it. I'll still wear them at work since they’re comfortable and breathable, but they're definitely not my favorite joggers, and I suggest you wear them with a tunic shirt.
