Warm and comfy I hiked 12.5 miles with an elevation change of about 3900 feet with many changes in weather and these leggings were perfect. Super warm when the wind was howling and not too hot when it got hot. The side pocket was big enough to hold a one liter bottle. My only complaint is they don’t seem to fit snug in the knee area. I am 5’4” 130 lbs and the small fits well.

So comfy! These are great! I got 4 different colors and love them. They fit wonderfully and are very flattering. The only con I can say if they aren’t *super* warm. They are fleece lined, so it’s great for a fall day. I did try them for a walk on a windy day that was around 37F-40F and my legs froze. My thighs were solid red when I got home and freezing to the touch.

Cold weather exercising I ordered two sizes (S/M) for comparison and plan to use these for cross country skiing and hiking in 25-35 degree weather when a wind layer isn’t necessary. 5’4” and 130 lbs. I tried both the small and medium. Both fit. The medium was a little loose in the waste and a little longer but I went with the medium in case they shrink in the wash. This is the same material and fit as the tights listed as “Thermal Winter Yoga Pants Warm Running Tights with Pockets”. The difference is that this product has a horizontal zipper pocket on the back of the waste band large enough to fit an iphone (photo). Nothing about them seem water resistant so I don’t expect them to be.

Awesome fleece yoga pants are super warm These are super warm and comfortable. The fleece lining is awesome, not too thick but very warm. The lining doesn’t seem to have affected the stretch which is excellent at 185%. The fabric stretches in all directionsI got the medium and they fit great, the compression amount is just what I wanted as I sized down. Normally, I would get the large but I intend on wearing these as a base layer under other winter gear and want them to be skin tight. For reference I am 40/36/39, 6’ tall and 184 pounds.Construction: These are well made; the fabric is thick and sewn well. Stitching looks precision and the flatlock seams are wonderful for keeping the bulk of the seam to a minimum. There is a crotch gusset that runs from one knee to the other completely disconnecting the leg and crotch seams and allowing for incredible movement without stress in the seams. Waistband is sturdy with good tension from a1/2” elastic band sewn into the top. The pocket in the rear waistband has a zipper and is lined with mess fabric. Unfortunately, the zipper doesn’t stretch at all and makes the waistband a little less elastic overall than one with no zipper.Overall: Great pants that will do what I need for my winter trip. The navy blue is beautiful, I would buy again.

Great for cooler days! These are nice quality fleece lined leggings. The fleece is not incredibly thick so works well for runs on cooler fall days. I was quite warm running in temps in the upper 30s. For temps below freezing they make a good baselayer under an outer layer. The high waistband sucks in your tummy area nicely as well. Because the waist band is nice and tight they were a little difficult to pull up over my hips. Once on, they fit great though! For reference I am 5'2" and weigh about 130. They do bunch around my ankles since I am not tall.

I live in Canada and was looking for some nice fleece pants. I ordered 4 different colors in XL. Three pairs showed up in XL and the purple pair was a L. I was a bit disappointed but tried them all on anyway. I usually wear a large in my clothing but based on reviews, I ordered the XL. It turns out both the L and XL for me well. The L is a bit tighter, but still fits. The XL pants fit perfect with a bot more room in the butt and upper thighs. I am going to keep all 4 pairs. They feel like really good quality and wash nicely. I let them hang to dry in case they shrink. The legs are a bit long and bunch at the ankles but it doesn’t bother me. I am 5ft 5 with an athletic build and a 30 inch inseam. I have a bit of a belly but not huge. My waist is about 34 inches and my thighs measure about 42inches. I love the fit and would recommend buying them to anyone. I purchased a medium pair for my niece as well and I am excited to give them to her for her Christmas present. Update-November 12, 2023- I have been wearing these leggings for almost a year now during cooler days. I have washed them in the washing machine and dried them in a dryer and they are still holding up. I love them. They haven’t shrunk or lost their shape and haven’t ripped or pilled. I still recommend these pants to anyone who asks. I am looking at purchasing the green pair, just so I have one in each color.

Comfortable and cute These weren’t what I expected at first, but after I put them on, I really liked them. They were very comfortable and looked ok for office pants. The buttons on the side class these up a bit. I thought they would be taper in at the ankle more, but they were more of a straight leg opening. The front pockets are deep and the back pockets are functional, just not very deep, my phone fell out when I bent over. The material is slightly thick, but these wash and dry well. They did shrink a tad after the first wash, but nothing too drastic and actually fit a bit better. These don’t have a belt loop, so the elastic waist is what you get. I ordered a medium and have been wearing size 8 lately. The medium fit fine and wasn’t too long or too short, just slightly loose around the waist at first, but better after wash. For reference, I am 5’4”, 141lbs, 30” waist, 28” inseam.

Accurate photos and description These are a stretchy polyester like thicker leggings would be. I was expecting something a little bit softer, but they are still comfortable and look fine. The style makes them look like regular dress pants. The front and back pockets are (somewhat) functional, and the waist detailing is cute (although I don’t usually tuck my shirts in so it won’t be seen). I ordered my usual Medium, as the size chart indicated I fell right in the middle of Medium measurements. I like the fit overall and they are comfortable. The waistband seems fine so far, but some belt loops would have been nice just in case they loosen up over time. Often pants labeled as “cropped” are a perfect fit on my short legs, but these are ankle pants on me, which I don’t think is the most flattering look. The 27” inseam is accurate on my Medium size pair. I would feel more comfortable if they were an inch longer or shorter, but that’s just me and my 29” inseam. They have straight slim legs (but not tight), so I think they will work tucked into boots, which solves the length problem for me. They seem like a nice quality, and I found the size chart to be accurate as well as the photos and description, so I feel I should give them the full 5 stars. I’ve included photos with a tape measure for inseam and waistband on the size Medium.

High-quality material and construction; sizing may be a problem We ordered these for my wife because she was looking for a warm pair of sweatpants to wear around the house in the winter. We looked at the sizing chart and ordered a size Small based on her measurements. She is 5’0” tall and weighs just under 100 pounds. The Small was too big around the waist and the hips, but the biggest problem was the length: it was way too long. The Small is what she is wearing in the two photos. We then ordered the Extra Small, thinking that would solve the problem. The Extra Small fit better in the waist and hips, but it was still at least 2” too long. We ended up sending it back for a refund.We started to rate these 3 stars, but decided that was too harsh because the material and workmanship in these sweatpants is really quite good. The material is very soft on the inside and very warm. The stitching is straight and well-done. These sweatpants have the feel of something that will last for a very long time. It could be that a person just needs to be 5’2” or taller to be able to wear them.

Soft, Warm, Stylish, and Perfect Length Most Amazon clothing tends to be a letdown, with odd fits that don't always work out. But every now and then, a piece surprises me—and these Fleece-Lined Sweatpants are a rare find.These sweatpants are thick and well-made without feeling overly heavy, providing the warmth I need. The interior has a soft, thick lining that feels incredibly comfortable against the skin. They feature an elastic waistband with a drawstring for adjustability and two practical side pockets. The back of the pants has a curved stitching just below the waistband, adding a subtle, flattering touch to the design. Additionally, a matching-color stripe down each leg adds a bit of style, setting them apart from typical sweatpants.The material includes some cotton, so they feel breathable even with the fleece lining. And the best part? The length. Finally, a pair of sweatpants that doesn’t look like I’m wearing something that shrank in the wash! At 5'7", they fall about half an inch from the ground without shoes—just the way I like it.At 128 pounds, I find these fit slightly loose, which I prefer for sweatpants. Absolutely my new favorite.

Nice fleece lined athletic pants I am a fan of fleece lined pants for the winter. These are many features to like about these sweatpants:1. The fleece lining is warm and soft. It’s not Sherpa so they are not overly warm.2. They run true to the size chart. I ordered the XL because I was in between the L and XL. I would rather have a little more room than have these be tight. They’re a little loose, but they are comfortable and don’t look oversized. They are also long enough! They are true to the 30” inseam.3. The sweatpants fabric is actually really nice. It’s closer to a ponte knit. I plan to not only use these for my walks in the winter, but also as casual wear with a sweater or jacket.4. The drawstring waist is substantial. And there are two side pockets.Overall, I’m very pleased with these. I may order a second pair as back up. They’re just as nice as similar items I’ve ordered from other brand name catalogs. They are a good value for the price.

Nice leggings These are great! Lots of pockets, including slip in cell phone sized, zippered for your valuables, and one in the back for your keys if you run in them. The fleece lining is soft and not too thick. I would not say these are super warm, but they’re warmer than a regular unlined pair of leggings. They are thick and nice enough to wear as pants. And they’re long! I’m 5’7” with a short torso and long legs, and these touch the floor by my heels.

Perfect fit and great quality at a great price Elastic-waisted pants are often hugely unflattering - but the cut, fabric, and drape of these create a flattering fit. I ordered the light gray in a L. I am 5'6" and ~ 145 lbs; these fit perfectly. They are great for loungewear, on the beach, working out, or running errands in spring/summer weather: the weight is light but substantial (not cheapy fabric) cotton. I am so surprised at how nice these are for the price.

These pants look great on me as long as I wear a top that covers my stomach and rear. I found them to be a bit tight in those areas as well as my upper thighs. I usually buy wide leg pants for this reason. These are straight cut. They are well made though and the fabric is thick and very soft on the inside. The waist fits great and is wide and comfortable. The length was perfect when I got them but they shrank about an inch when I washed and dried them on normal settings. If you’re shorter than 5’6” these would be a good length. The pockets are on the small side but do fit my cellphone. These are casual pants but could work in an office setting if you wear a nice top. They’re great for lounging at home.

Inaccurate fabric description I’m pretty picky about sweatpants and sweatshirts being 100% cotton or at least above 90% cotton with a bit of spandex or elastine for stretch. These appeared to fit the bill and I was so disappointed to see that they are a cotton poly blend with spandex. The fit is okay-I’m 5’5” 128lbs and medium was not too tight not too loose, although I like joggers baggy so a large might be better. I’m returning these as fabric feels thin and slightly scratchy and I the entire reason I avoid cotton poly blends is because they pill so easily.

I purchased these looking to replace scrub pants that are no longer available at my local store (that I've been wearing almost daily due to weight gain). These show as 92% cotton on the tag so that's great. They are very soft like a yoga pants and drape nicely so that I can wear them out anywhere as well. I'm currently holding most of my weight in my belly so the elastic waist is nice as well as the fact that they're not low rise. Just wearing them around today, they've stretched a little bit so they're less tight looking than yoga pants (which is a bonus). Nice to find pants for a bigger girl that doesn't produce the dreaded muffin top effect lol. Will 100% buy more of these once I've dropped some weight! For reference, I'm 5'8" and weight 205lbs. I bought an XL based on other reviews and they're perfect sizing including the length on my 31.5 inseam.

Comfortable and cute I’m 5’4 and weigh 135 pounds and am wearing a small. These pants are so cute and comfortable. This is my second pair. I originally ordered a pair in khaki and liked them so much I ordered another pair. It is a medium weight material, a little too heavy for summer but great for all the other seasons. They wash well and the corners on the pockets don’t curl up and it is minimal wrinkling. I have washed them several times and I put them in the dryer with everything else. The size is true. If I were to translate it to a number size I would say it is a size 6. If you are over 5’5 they might be too short. I love the ties on the bottom to change the style from straight leg to more of a jogger. The only downside is the ties don’t always stay tied so if you are wearing them straight leg style, they can drag on the ground.

Strange, long length Overall, I would say that these pants are average, which is why I am giving them an average 3-star rating. The main reason I'm not a huge fan of them is because they are a weird length. In the photos they look like a capri length, but they're much longer than that. I'm tall (5'11"), and they are as long as pants on me. So if you're taller they might be cropped/capri length, but if you're shorter they would probably be too long even for pants. The material is quite thin and see through. It doesn't have much stretch to it at all as it's almost like a chiffon type material, so I would definitely size up if you want them to fit flowy and loose as pictured. I sized up two from my regular size, and they fit well in the waist and legs. Finally, although it says they are ivory white in color, I would say they are more of a tan color. I don't mind the color though.

These are nice pants. I consider the fabric to be a linen, crepe like polyester that is comfortable to wear. The size is accurate according to given size chart. I often take a 3xl in bottoms, and I got a 3xl in these. They got loosely, as they are supposed to fit I believe. I suppose, it would be possible to fit into a smaller size if you are on lower end of size chart as these are generously sized. I am 5’8” and they come stop above my ankles, do a little cropped on me. The legs are wide and loose. I tend to get black in most pants, but got the gray in these, expecting more of a cotton like fabric. Although the gray color is accurate, I wish I had got black because I feel I could dress them up and get more wear. The elastic waistband on these is just right as far as comfort.

These are GORGEOUS pants! Just wish they were slightly longer! Worth the $! These are unlike any other pants that I own, which is a very good thing. My only gripe is that i wish they were a tad longer. This happens to me a lot, but being that I am only 5’6’’, I am not TALL, but it happens very frequently, so i don’t dry any of my pants, pretty much, in the dryer for this reason. ANYWAY, I like to be able to wear a small wedge or platform heel. These i can only wear with my sneaks. But it could be worse, they could be more cropped, so they are for sure wearable, but i’ll hang to dry. If you are taller, make note of this. I mean, I won’t give these lower than 5 stars because they’re perfect. Got that sheen, as i show in my pic. I will try to take a photo wearing them soon. I have no help at the moment to do so. They are thick enough (no worries about being see through), without being so thick that they weigh you down & are too heavy for the springtime. They’re PERFECT in weight, style, comfort- everything! They’re unique, fun & can be worn casually, or for a night on the town. I have no other pair like it, so that intrigued me. They have an elastic back, which i LOVE, and it makes it forgiving (size wise). No worries if you gain a few lbs. or have a bloated day. I could have even gone with an XS, if they had it, due to the stretch. {I’m 5’6’’, 129 lbs., 27.5 waist, 36 inch hips. Small fits, and has a bit of room to spare, so you may wish to size down on this one, due to the stretch.} If you’re tinier, the rise will just be lower. 5+ stars! I’d love other colors, as I am a bit too old for the hot pink one. I love the shade, but doubt I’ll be bold enough to wear pants that hue, but hey.. we’ll see. I never say never. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ ** I hope my review has been 😊👍🏻. **

My favorite jeans! I love these retro hippy jeans! I had to send a few pairs back, but now I have a size that fits just right. For reference I’m 5’7” 150lbs with a thick middle and thin legs. I’m 59 y/o but tend to shop in the juniors department because of my legs being thin. I’m a woman’s size 10 so that’s what I ordered at first. These jeans run large in the middle and they’re very stretchy. I sent them back and got a size 8. They were also a bit too roomy in the middle. They fit snug, but I could stretch the waist out several inches. Pushing my luck, I sent them back and got a size 6. This girl hasn’t been a size 6 since the 70’s! They fit and felt great. The waist is not too tight to expose a muffin top and actually still has a bit of room if I stretch it out. Posting photos to show measurements. If you have thicker legs, there is still plenty of stretch in these pants as long as you like them snug. The length was perfect even after I machine washed them in cold water. I hung to dry overnight then put in the dryer for 5 minutes just to soften up. Directions say hand wash, but I raised five kids so I never had time for such nonsense :)

Waistband is small! These pants are super adorable, and very nice quality. I absolutely LOVE the pink color. However, here are the issues I have with these pants: 1. The waistband is soooo tight. I stretched them out on the back of a chair for two days hoping it would make the elastic give a little. I even steamed it while stretched but neither gave me much, if any relief. 2. I am tall, 5'9" and pants are always short on me. I can make these long enough by pulling them down from my waist (which then makes the elastic even tighter when lowered to my hip) but I have a lot of sagging in the crotch. I will still wear these but will have to wear them with a long silk shirt that will cover up the crotch area. 3. Last things to know are that you should NOT wear any lotion of any kind when wearing these pants. I mistakenly had some on when I wanted to wear these and this fabric really shows moisture so I couldn't wear them and had to send them to the wash. Which, MAKE SURE YOU WASH THEM SEPARATELY. The pink dye in these pants bled onto other items in my wash. Nothing was important so I didn't care but everyone should know and take heed of this warning. Anyway, super cute pants. I may just take the elastic out completely (if that is possible) and make it a drawstring waist because I DO want to wear these.

Updated design, terrible quality I’ve been buying the Just My Size leggings, carpri-length leggings and biker shorts for years. This year, they changed the pattern: there’s a thicker waist band and very large seams down the legs and around the waist. As someone who works from home, these are an every day staple for me. Additionally, I have a lot of sensory issues with clothes and these rarely bother me. I was disappointed to see the thicker seams and waistband when I received them. I was in major need of some new pairs though since all mine are very faded and worn, so I decided to give them a try. They’re still pretty comfortable, but I feel the thicker seams more on my stomach. For reference, I’m 5’2 so they come up pretty high on my waist. My biggest complaint is that I received these pants on May 15 and didn’t even start wearing them right away but they’re already falling apart. The internal seams have started unraveling on both pairs. I tried to sew them back to keep them from falling apart more. But then I noticed the whole outside of the waist is also unraveling. I know just enough about sewing to get by, but not enough to repair an entire waistband. I feel so disappointed and like these were a waste of money. I would greatly appreciate it if Amazon or the manufacturer could provide reimbursement for these defective pants.

Not your COMMON sweatpants Just ok. I'm 5'3" and about 145 lbs, and i bought a size large. They fit as I like these type of pants to fit. Loose ,but not dragging on the ground. These pants I consider to be "lounge" pants and not sweatpants. I don't really care for the material used. The outer material is rough and has ribbed lines that make it feel kind of slick and is a bit shiny. The inside is flat with slight ribbing, but I wouldn't exactly call it soft. I put on leggings underneath them because I didn't care for the material next to my skin, plus the thin weight of the material required it for warmth. However, the inside is not as rough as the outside of the pants. The elastic on the ankles is loose and the adjustment knob drags on the ground, hanging loose, ( not sure how long it will last like that?). I do like that the waist drawstring is on the inside of the pants and won't be hanging down in front. If regular fleece sweatpants are too heavy for you,and you want something different than the normal sweatpants out there, these will work for you. If you are looking for a warm comfy, snuggly pair of sweatpants, that will keep you warm through the cold, keep looking. These pants will not work for that. Maybe especially good for summer wear?

Comfortable, but not necessarily flattering I LOVE these pants. At this point, I own about 8 pairs. They are my go-to 'sitting around at home' pant and I wear them almost every day. They're thin enough to wear in hot weather, but aren't sheer, and the waistband is great (where it cinches in pretty far but can stretch a ton, in the XL it's 15 inches across the waistband un-stretched, and 30 inches when stretched all the way out). There are a few things that I would make a note of, though. Firstly, the different colors have different qualities. The grey is my favorite, it's the stretchiest and softest. The black is stiffer and thicker (but still a go-to for me), and the green is sheerer and more like an upscale legging or almost similar to a thin dress pant (and is my least favorite). These pants can also be quite unflattering. This is because the fit is a little weird (which is sort of expected with 'parachute' pants) but isn't my favorite. I am a little over 200lbs with a mildly athletic build (like, 'athlete who's let themselves go for a few years'), and primarily have these in size XL. The inseam is one of the main issues for me. I have normal length of leg, if not slightly short, where the inside of my leg is about 31 inches long. The inseam for these pants is 24.5 inches long, and there is about 15 inches between the end of the inseam and the top of the waistband. In order to get rid of that extra room, I roll the waistband 1-2x over, so that they generally look and fit like normal pants and so my legs to stick together when I walk. this makes the pants hit me in a weird place, though, like lower calf. The pants also tend to be thin enough to show, if you are not very thin/toned, an unfortunate texture on the thighs. Between these factors, the pants are so ugly even my friends and family have commented that these pants need to stay as house pants and never be worn in public. The only other issue with these is that they do stretch out when you wear them, so they'll be pretty tight for 2-3 wears, then get really loose, then go back to being tight after you wash them. They also wear in certain places and eventually get holes, but it takes a while to get to that point. In general, I find the grey and black versions of this to be amazing for working at home, doing chores, or laying around (or even sleeping), but are not great for going out. They're super comfortable and work in most types of mild weather, and because of that, I plan to continue buying more of these in the future.

Comfortable, but not necessarily flattering I LOVE these pants. At this point, I own about 8 pairs. They are my go-to 'sitting around at home' pant and I wear them almost every day. They're thin enough to wear in hot weather, but aren't sheer, and the waistband is great (where it cinches in pretty far but can stretch a ton, in the XL it's 15 inches across the waistband un-stretched, and 30 inches when stretched all the way out). There are a few things that I would make a note of, though. Firstly, the different colors have different qualities. The grey is my favorite, it's the stretchiest and softest. The black is stiffer and thicker (but still a go-to for me), and the green is sheerer and more like an upscale legging or almost similar to a thin dress pant (and is my least favorite). These pants can also be quite unflattering. This is because the fit is a little weird (which is sort of expected with 'parachute' pants) but isn't my favorite. I am a little over 200lbs with a mildly athletic build (like, 'athlete who's let themselves go for a few years'), and primarily have these in size XL. The inseam is one of the main issues for me. I have normal length of leg, if not slightly short, where the inside of my leg is about 31 inches long. The inseam for these pants is 24.5 inches long, and there is about 15 inches between the end of the inseam and the top of the waistband. In order to get rid of that extra room, I roll the waistband 1-2x over, so that they generally look and fit like normal pants and so my legs to stick together when I walk. this makes the pants hit me in a weird place, though, like lower calf. The pants also tend to be thin enough to show, if you are not very thin/toned, an unfortunate texture on the thighs. Between these factors, the pants are so ugly even my friends and family have commented that these pants need to stay as house pants and never be worn in public. The only other issue with these is that they do stretch out when you wear them, so they'll be pretty tight for 2-3 wears, then get really loose, then go back to being tight after you wash them. They also wear in certain places and eventually get holes, but it takes a while to get to that point. In general, I find the grey and black versions of this to be amazing for working at home, doing chores, or laying around (or even sleeping), but are not great for going out. They're super comfortable and work in most types of mild weather, and because of that, I plan to continue buying more of these in the future.

Mixed Fit; Skip Ceylon I’ve owned the black pair for almost a year now and I love them! The fabric isn’t sweatshirt, but it’s still decently warm (I’ve been able to wear it year-round). Good for working out indoors or lounging. I have no reason to believe it’s not 100% cotton and I’m a sewist. Im 5’5”, 29” waist and the medium hits at my ankle and feel comfortable (not tight or loose) around my waist. I think the problem with these is the don’t look anything like what’s pictured. They aren’t overly baggy in pant leg width or length, nor are they fluffy. Now, I did have a big problem with the Ceylon color one. In the picture, it looks like a grey-green, but it’s actually a light, bright sea green in-person. Terrible color on me. I would have overlooked it if they hadn’t also been 3” too short! Disappointing since I have to drive 30 minutes to make returns. Skip this color unless you’re VERY petite!

Also, the dark grey color I ordered wasn’t as advertised- The ones I got were much lighter. I will be returning these. I’m 5’2”, 26.5” waist, and 36” hips and I ordered a MED, which were a nice and relaxed fit.

Great Pants, Long Shipping Would've gave 5 stars, but they took around a month to get to me. Other than that, they are exactly what was pictured. I'm not really a fan of the fake back & zipper pockets, but the cargo pockets are pretty big & I can fit almost my whole hand in the top side pockets. I'm also glad I went with the extra large, because I feel like they're made small & they aren't made with a stretchy material. A large definitely wouldn't have fit. For reference, I'm 4'11", roughly 160lbs & I think my waist is around 35"-36" so I'm a bit chunky in the midsection. They are also a little long, but they will rest on my shoes so that's okay. If you're 5'4 maybe 5'5 I think the length would be perfect. They're also high waisted, which I also like. If you prefer hip huggers or low rise, the crotch might hang near your knees😂 All in all they're nice, they're comfortable & they aren't thick which is nice because they are for work. Breathable is better. Hope this review was , I tried to be as detailed as possible :-)

Perfect Pants for the Holidays These tartan plaid pants are really cute! The perfect pant for Christmas celebrations. The red color is vibrant, and the pattern is really beautiful. The big bow that ties in the front adds festive style! I found them to be plenty long enough for my long legs (I normally wear 32 inch inseam jeans). They did run a touch snug though in the waist, so if you are between sizes in that area you may want to size up. There was a lot of extra room in the hip area though. I like the cut with the gathered waistline and loose, wide leg. Very on trend currently. The fabric was different than I expected. It is heavier and a bit more stiff than other dress pants in my closet. It feels like a quality fabric that should last. I do wish that there were pockets. For that missing element, I took off one star. All told, I am quite pleased with these fun red plaid pants. Looking forward to wearing them at the family holiday celebration!

The waist band (button - no elastic) fit me fine. Length and rise were ok/good (I typically wear Petite 2/4 in brands like Loft). Hips and thighs were so big. The fabric is thin which gives these pants a pajama bottom look due to the poor fit. Maybe these are perfect for someone who is petite and curvy? That is not me. For reference, I am 5'3" - 125# - 36B with an athletic/boyish build.

Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

Unexpectedly in love! So, let me start off by saying they don’t look as good in the pictures as I feel they do in real life. I am 5’9” and between 175-180 depending on the week of the month 🙄. Anyway! I LOVE these. It’s so hard for me to find pants that don’t give me camel toe because one of my hips is a little higher up than the other. But these pants fit so great. I thought they’d be a little longer, but I really don’t need to wear heels at all with these. I actually have to wear them a little lower than I wanted because they don’t go as long as I’d like - I wanted them to sweep the floor honestly. So I’d say, definitely size up bc I’m usually a large in pants and also definitely buy these pants !

I actually really like these I’m a vet tech student so I end up spending a lot of time bending down, sitting on my knees, walking around, etc. I need a lot of room in my scrubs in order to move and not show my butt to the whole world. With that being said, this material does not allow a lot of stretch, like at all. So if you’re iffy about sizes, just go up a size. I bought two pairs of scrubs: one set in XL and another in XXL. The XL fit most comfortably, although they can be a little snug in the thigh/butt region. I’m a larger, curvy gal with thunder thighs so I’m really not shocked. Also, the pants are a little too long for my 5’5” stature, so they’re gonna have to be hemmed to stop them from dragging on the floor constantly. The XXL set was so big, however, I was practically swimming in them. It was unflattering, so I returned them for a full refund. Now, back to the material: it’s mostly just polyester. No cotton or spandex. It’s a bit thick so yes, it does get hot (gotta love that +100 degree Cali weather). However, the plus side to the material is hair just comes right off it! Which is so exciting for me (haha). So if you work with animals all day, like I do, then you don’t need to carry around a lint roller all the time with these scrubs. Also, there are so many pockets! Love it! I’m gonna give these scrubs the seal of approval.

user_comment: Awesome! So I literally just got these in the mail, ignore the wrinkles. But these pants are great!! I am 5'4", roughly 120 lbs, and I ordered the XS. The fit is almost perfect! Fits as expected, plenty of pockets (none zippered, though.) I'm 5'6" and 145lbs. In pants, I fit somewhere between a size 10 and 12 (US). I bought the black pants in a size Large, and I'd say it's true to size as it's a bit big around the waist and a tiny bit long (which I prefer over too short!), but I'm fairly certain the Medium would be too small for me (which is in sync with the size guide.) I find them comfortable. So far, about 8 months in, they've held up to hot-water machine washes 1-3x/week without fading or distorting. I just bought a second pair and can't tell the difference. Just FYI: There are hot pink accents on the black pants, which I didn't see in the advertised photos. I bought these for volunteering at an animal shelter. I needed something for my phone, cat treats, dog treats, gloves, poop bags, etc. The multiple pockets are handy for this. I wish there was a zippered, deep pocket for my phone, but so far, it's sat in the top (unzippered) pockets without falling out. (And at a shelter, you're always moving, bending, etc.) These DO gather animal fur and lint up quickly, so be aware. I used to be a paramedic, as well, and these pants seem like they'd be good for healthcare. I could easily stuff multiple emergency items in these, though like I said above, a zippered pocket or two would be nice for extra security of personal or important items. If you need to carry more than a couple things, these beat out traditional scrub bottoms by a long shot.

But my joy turned to disappointment after I put them on after the first wash and dry. they were now frustratingly just short enough to look and feel awkward. I laid them out ontop of the new unwashed pair and sure enough, they were about 2.5 inches shorter! So disappointing. I really hope the manufacturer reads this and does something to correct it in the future for all of us big and tall women out there struggling to find good clothes.If it were not for the shrinkage I would defintiely gives these pants 5 stars.

A Review from a girl who Actually Collects These Styles of Pants Hi! So okay, I have had a few pairs of these “soft, boho pattern flare pants” throughout my young life. I remember when they peaked back in early 2010s! So, if you see my photos, I already owned a few pairs in Red/Pink and Black/White. These pants are my favorite. They are so soft, such good quality. They feel like cotton, they’re pretty thick (and don’t show panty lines!), and they’re the perfect length on my 5’7 self, they are truly the best. I really wanted some in Blue, but I couldn’t find any in my area. So I decided to give Amazon a shot. I’m a bit disappointed. You may not be able to tell from my photos, but these pants are… different. The pattern is clearly laser printed. It doesn’t look as ‘real’ as the red and black ones. Also, the quality is much worse. It doesn’t feel like soft cotton, it feels more like polyester. The pattern does look good, but it’s just not the same. The fit is fine, but it’s more lightweight than my genuine boho pants. It’s also more see through!?! That’s not supposed to happen, IMO. Overall, they’re thinner, not as soft, and really cheaply made. But still, If you’re just going for a boho look, then go for it. I’d say, if you can, try to find the real deal boho flares in discount department stores.

Slim fit, great pockets I expected these to fit like sweatpants. In the front they kind of do but in the back, they have a very slim fit. I thought this made them hang really nice, mire like dress slacks, but it might be challenging for someone who is bigger through the hips and buttocks region. Despite being a knit fabric there’s not a whole lot of stretch to the material. The material is very soft and comfortable. The pockets are amazing. There are six packets including two cargo on the sides small pockets on the back and deep pockets for your hands upfront. I washed these and they didn’t have any color fade or pilling. Seem to be wearing well. For the right figure, I think these are an excellent find. They were long enough for my 5 foot seven frame, and it would be ideal for someone who is slimmer through the hips and butt, frustrating if you’re bigger in those areas. A little poochy in the front like a sweat pant but overall very nice. I think they will be a comfortable option for travel- look nicer than sweatpants, have pockets for storage, and will be comfortable.

Not the fabric I was expecting These are so close to what I wanted. I’m 5’4” about 115lbs and ordered the size 4. Bought these for a hiking/backpacking trip. While standing, I thought the fit was perfect and very flattering. The pockets were deep and functional. However these ended up going back because the material was a very thick canvas type and had ZERO stretch so when I moved or sat down my ankles immediately became exposed and it wasn’t comfortable, they were also fairly heavy for hiking pants. The type of material also collected a lot of lint, not a huge deal because i wasn’t going to wear them for fashion, but I only had these out of the bag long enough to try them on and take some photos and you can see all the fuzz they picked up. (The bleachy red tint in the last photo is an error from my camera, the pants were solid black). If these were made out of a more stretchy material I would have loved to keep them. The cut, style, and pockets were exactly what I was looking for, I was disappointed to have to return.

Hurray! Palazzo pants that come in short and plus sizes! As a 2X plus size and just 5'4", most palazzo or wide leg pants I've ordered have been far too long. I am thrilled to find these classy, elegant, flattering palazzo pants that come in plus AND short sizes. They run generously sized, as would be expected with palazzo pants. VERY comfortable but thin, so plan to wear them with tunics unless you have seamless thongs and nothing to hide Those jeggings are true-to-size (I'm 5'5", 125 lbs, wear size 8 pants. I ordered the Medium and it's perfect), very stretchy, well made, and extremely comfortable. They're also pretty versatile as the leg bottom can be folded out (without looking bad) to wear as capris. However, as a few customers have mentioned, they're quite thin, so plan to wear them with long shirts/tunics because every single panty line and bulge will show. Also noteworthy: The buttons up front and on the back pockets are studded with "diamonds". Unless you're into the fake-diamond look, that's yet another reason to wear those with long shirts/tunics. The choice of colors, comfort and price make them a pretty attractive option (hence the 4 stars), but keep in mind they may target to a younger, on-the-thin-side crowd...

Good for the price. Could be better. I got the plum colored pants in size medium. I am 5'6" and I typically wear a size 4-6. The length on these pants was just right. I'm unsure how I feel about the fit of the pants on me, but I'd say these are comparable to a 2-4 sized pant. I wanted these to look like skinny jeans, but feel like leggings. For the most part, they accomplish that. The fabric is thin, soft, and stretchy, which is good for physical comfort, but also means that the fabric really conforms to your body and doesn't feel like it's hiding anything. For this reason, I'd personally stick to darker colors that might not show every dimple off.

No size standards whatsoever These are SO comfortable. The length is just right (I am 5’4”) However, you have to play a guessing game on the size. I purchased the black/blue combo pack in XL. They are both labeled XL, but the blue pants are GIGANTIC. I am not a small woman but the blue ones were way too big in the waist. The black ones fit perfectly. I can’t imagine why the factory doesn’t use some sort of size standard, but I think it comes from China and they don’t have standards so you just have to hope you’re getting what you ordered. I like the black ones though. I’ll be wearing them often! EDIT: One year later... The fabric is thin and the seams ripped due to the stitching creating a "perforated line" that splits the fabric, especially in the inner thigh. The stitching itself seems okay, I don't think the thread they used was the problem. It's the thin fabric.

Love these need more! So I recently switched jobs and now have to dress business casual. As someone tall and thin 5’8.5” I have the hardest time finding pants that fit especially in the length let alone online shopping. Perfect length for me to wear with either open toe heels, sandals or the right pair of boots/booties. I’m wearing a size Medium in the turquoise. Just a suggestion for your undies to wear something smooth and possibly nude or white as you can see pattern material through the front (see circled photo).

SO WARM, AMAZING These pants are awesome. Exactly like the description, fit me perfect (ordered XS - 5’5” 125 lb) and so warm/comfortable I never want to take them off. They are also a true black color, not an off black.

Comfy, not flattering I’m 5’1”, around 165 pounds & ordered a L. The pants are several inches too long on me but wearable cuffed or not due to the elastic ankles. The pants are comfortable & warm. I walked the dog in them, at about 25 F without layering & was fine. They fall down a bit on me, despite the waist cord, but a smaller size would expose too much behind if I bent over. Given how these look, there’s no excuse for the shallow pockets. They ought to help deep pockets but they don’t- pockets aren’t even deep enough for a phone. Arrived very quickly. Very cozy. I do recommend them. But don’t plan on using the pockets & don’t plan on feeling pretty. If you’re short, do plan on them being bunched.

Nice, thick and really warm I was really surprised...They Fit Perfectly! I bought these pants to wear when I remove the snow and in temps below 36°, I live in a Northern state in the U.S near Canada, so you know that we are cold which is now 34° out. I stand at 5,1" and weight 118 lbs, I normally weara petite small to some medium like a 6p in a stretch fabric paint and a size 2/4 in misses size pants a size 2 if it has stretch in it. I bought these fleece lined pants on the flash deal in a size small and for me being Petite, I thought that they would be loose, and long, but guys, they were not! I do not have long legs just advantage length and eventho I am small these pants are thick and I don't have to shorten them or roll them up, my inseam is a 29" with flats on. I wished that I would have bought more than one pair. If you have a child in college, in the cold these are perfect for the campus walking and catching the bus and yes, walking in the cold, those 2000 steps and for shoveling snow, I feel no cold. I know that I am going to have to Wash them in wool lite and hung dry to keep the heavy fleece lining good and not twisted and to keep them from shrinkage. The fit is perfect!

Perfect lounge pants! These are fabulous. They’re thick, soft, loose, and the sage color is beautiful. If you’re looking for something that will make you look thin - this is not your pant. If you’re looking for something to lounge, watch movies, eat bon bons, and stay warm in… this is a winner in my book. I am 135lb and 5’8”, and I ordered a large. I prefer a looser fit with my lounge pants but if I wanted a more snug fit I could’ve gone with medium. See pics for how the large fits me. They’re also long, and go all the way to my ankles even when I’m sitting with legs crossed - big plus in my book. The only thing I’d make better is deeper pockets - why do clothing manufacturers never consider that women actually keep anything in their pockets?? I can’t even fit my whole hand in one of them. I haven’t washed these yet but I’ll change my review afterwards if anything goes amiss there. They seem like they’ll wash well tho. Edit - had these for several months now and still going strong!! They wash well and are still as warm and cozy.

I am (5'2" - 145lbs) a person who feels cold a lot. Bought these in medium. These keep me so warm and comfy. I have worn them outside when temps are below 32 degrees and windy and the cold didn't bother me. They also make me look like I have elephant legs but that matters so much less than comfort for me. If you are trying to keep heating cost low but want to be warm and comfy, these should be a serious consideration.

Incredibly thick fleece I am an older woman, 5’1”, 145 lbs and have a short torso. I normally wear a size 10 pant. I bought large because I wanted comfortable pants for lounging in the evenings. I keep my thermostat at 63 degrees. These are comfortably warm. I’m pretty sure that a size medium would be a bit more flattering, but as thick s the fleece lining is, they probably would be. Bit awkward. There is a lot of bulk in the crotch area. The fabric is soft. I haven’t washed them yet, but they look like they will shed. Hopefully not too much. The only negative is the pockets are shallow. I pulled the waist rope, it is a bit tacky. If you want warm pants, I would recommend these.

Perfect fit I’m writing a review for the fit. I’m 5’2, 135 and ordered a small. I looked at so many different pants before I decided on these. Im pear shaped so I have a really hard time finding bottoms in person let alone guessing when I order online. They’re either too tight in the thigh/butt or a huge gap at the waist. These fit just right. I will say I’m curious how they will hold up as the label says to hand wash….. not sure if I missed that in the description but nobody has time for that. I’ll add to my review once it gets worn Update- wore these to Denver when it was 18 degrees with windchill at night. They definetly did the job. Haven’t washed them yet but I hope the fleece stays puffy

10/10 recommend & i will definitely be buying more. I got these pants as an early xmas gift & when i tell you them came just in time. We just had a wicked snowstorm with 50mph winds and temperatures below 0.. these pants didn’t disappoint. Theyre unbelievably cozy and kept me perfectly warm (at least my legs). These are for sure my new go-to cold weather pants. i’m 5’3 and 107 pounds and the small fit great with plenty of extra stretch & leg space left. I will say the waistband tends to twist up a little when washed but it’s easy to flip back into place. The fleece stayed soft and i didn’t notice any shrinkage after the wash and dry no noticeable shedding after wearing them over clothes

Very fluffy… I am 5’10”, size 6, about 150 lbs., athletic build. I’d normally order a medium but most mediums are not long enough for me, they go to my ankle, if that, with that said I ordered a large for the length. I ordered them for warmth, not fashion, I walk my dog super early in the mornings and at times it’s super chilly. I don’t think that I could move enough in these to walk my dog, they’d get all jacked up and be in the way. I look like the state puff marshmallow man in them! Granted they are super warm and the inside “fur” is very soft but the thickness of them is a little much for me. Maybe a medium wouldn’t be as bad but for the price I don’t think I’d use them often enough so I’m returning them. I hope the photos help!

Thick fleece! The fleece in these is definitely thicker than your average fleece-lined pant. On the bright side, that makes them toasty warm. But on the other hand they sort of make you look like the Michelin man. I've washed and dried them once and the fluff factor was average, only a tiny bit of pilling. I'm 5'00, 106lbs and bought the XS. They fit well. I'm not sure these would work well for tall people.

Not Flattering, Feels like Felt I ordered these Sherpa Sweatpants for winter. They arrived shrinkwrapped and hard as a board. When I cut the package open they softened but are very wrinkled. The fabric is unusual, it feels like a slightly softer version of felt. The sewing seems fair, but overall the product seems inexpensively made. I ordered a small. I am a size 6 in pants, 118 pounds, and 5 feet tall. They fit, though they are a little small in the bum and have extra fabric around the tummy/front crotch area. If you are looking for a flattering, SEXY pair of sweatpants THESE ARE NOT WHAT YOU ARE LOOKING FOR. They are on the other hand very warm and soft. Color is what I expected. Mine are already stained and I have only owned them a week. Wups. You can not put them in the dryer fyi. The pockets are tiny (see photo).

I bought these pants just for lounging at home on cold days without having to crank up the heat. Yes they are super baggy but I didn’t get them for looks. I got a size small. I’m 5’3”, 130lbs, with relatively short legs. The length is pretty good for me, and the ankles are not too tight. I had originally intended to cut the cuffs but there’s no need. The waistline fits fine, but would have liked a bit more relaxed fit, since I’m just lounging in them. Threw out the drawstring, not needed and it’s ugly. A size medium might have been better for waistline, but I think would have been too big everywhere else. Very comfortable and warm. Would not recommend running or exercising in them as they will get too hot.

NOT what everyone raves about I wanted to love these like so many people do, but these simply aren’t the same quality many have claimed. Maybe they’ve skimped on new batches since they are so popular? I got them in January 2022. Here are my pros and cons. Unfortunately there are too many cons for me to like these leggings. For reference, I am 116 lbs and 5’4”. I got a medium. Pros -They are buttery soft, like everyone says. -They have pockets. -They were $16 Cons -The quality of the material is sub par. They are thin to the point that if they were any other color than “dark”, they could be see through. -They aren’t made well. I’m wondering how long the seams will hold. -The fabric bled when I got them. I tried them on and put my hands in the pockets. My hands, fingernails and legs had black dye on them! -I washed them (with a color catcher sheet to protect my other dark clothes) and they faded on the first wash. -One leg is more than an inch longer than the other. I’m 5’4” and had to roll one leg up 3 times and the longer one 4 times. -The logo on the back was faded and “crumbly” upon arrival. It was already peeling off. I’m keeping them only because returning them is more of a hassle than keeping a $16 pair of leggings. I won’t buy more. I’ll wear them to work in the yard or possibly the gym, but definitely not for anything else. They aren’t flattering to me. They are so thin and remind me of tights. I hope this info helps you! I always read reviews before buying anything in Amazon.

These are a must! I'm terrible at taking pics. But I got some of the pants, the length, and with an entire outfit. I hope they help. For reference, I am 5'0" and a out 130lbs (changes between 128-133 often, because well... food) anyway..... If you have any type of belly weight, I would recommend sizing up. Hence the bathroom pics b3cause I don't have full length mirrors at home.

Good but not great For reference, I ordered an XL/31" inseam based on the sizing chart - I can't confirm if they're actually XL since the tag looks like it was chewed away or something. The pants are very comfortable and fit well, they are just not made that well. As others have noted, they're covered in lint/hair, so keep a lint roller handy. I loved that this has front and back pockets, but the front pockets weren't sewn properly so there are holes in them. I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

Best Work Pants EVER!!! I don't normally leave reviews, but I absolutely had to for these pants. I am a server at a very busy restaurant, in Florida where it gets hot as heck. I was out of work for 2 months thanks to covid shutting down all our restaurants, and well I gained some weight while sitting at home doing nothing 🤣🤣🤣. I was called to return to work on Memorial Day weekend and of course, my black work pants would not fit over my newly found, voluptuous body. I spent 2 days running from store to store, fitting rooms were closed and I was getting desperate. I bought and returned no less than 10 pairs to various stores. I found 2 pairs that fit decently, but I absolutely hated because they didn't fit right. I have a small waist, big hips, big thighs, etc., so I need more of a stretchy material. I gave in and hit Amazon figuring if I had to buy and return multiple pairs, I could at least do it in the comfort of my own home. Well, this was the 1st pair i ordered. I can absolutely say, i LOVE LOVE these! They fit perfect, stretch in all the right places, they are light enough so they aren't hot, so soft, unlike starchy, rough dress pants. If you have a job where you are required to wear black dress pants, I highly recommend these. For reference, sizing is spot on. I am 5'7", 165 lbs and typically wear a size 11 juniors/8 or 10 womens. I bought the Medium 31" inseam and they fit perfectly! I ordered 2 more pairs right away!

I don't know how they hold as I've only had them a week, but I'm super happy with how stretchy and comfy they are, and that I'm not constantly fussing with a zipper or yanking my pants up. I am 5'8 on a good day and 188 pounds and dropping, and bought the XL/33". I actually feel these are an inch or so too long for me, but I fold them up so they don't drag. I also feel I could've gone with a large and plan on my next pair being a L/31" as I continue to lose weight. ***Updating*** These lasted a good while and I still have them, but found I do definitely prefer the more expensive Betabrand Pants. I'm now 174 and the XL are too baggy. I did end up ordering a L/31 & they fit better.

I thought these would be a good length for me since I’m barely 5’0” but I would have preferred them longer. Also these are not high waisted and if I try to pull them up even a little higher it looks really unflattering in the crotch area. Underwear lines show through so I can only wear thongs with them and I wore them once around the house and they developed lint balls near the crotch. They also have a long line down the thigh (see pic). Haven’t washed them yet and not too hopeful. The style is cute though, soft, and you can wear these out and look put together but for the price I’m just eh about them and wouldn’t get them again. I also tried the 27” inseam and the length was good on those but the ends were too wide and looked funny so I returned them. When you purchase these pants it even warns it’s a frequently returned item.

Love that that you can order by inseam! I have been looking for comfortable lined pants with pockets to wear for Wisconsin winter walking for awhile and finally found a these. I ordered them in 35" inseam and they were too long! I exchanged them for the 33" inseam and am so pleased with the fit and comfort. I didn't realize when I bought them that they also had a drawstring at the bottom so you can cinch them at ankle when walking in sloppy winter/spring conditions.(see pic) They are perfect.... just what I was looking for!

So cute! I’m 5 foot 2, and these are actually the right length for once! I’ve tried several different palazzo pants, and these are the best. They were a bit long at first, but shrunk in the wash to the perfect size!

Great for us petite ladies! Very comfortable yoga pants in a pretty blue color. The detail at the waist is a nice touch! These are great if you’re petite like me (5’3”) since the cuffs are so high. The material is nice and thick and properly stretchy.

Great work pants I am 5’9 and 220lbs. I bought the XL and they fit perfect. They are buttery smooth and do not hold wrinkles. I can easily bend in squat down without any binding from the waistband. These pants are the perfect dress casual attire. They have pockets that are deep enough for my Iphone. Would recommend.

Buttery Soft, perfect for activity, leisure, and nice enough to dress up. Overall I was super pleased with this purchase! As someone who is 5’2 and somewhat “built” (150ish lb but I lift) I STRUGGLE to find pants that work in both length and stretch. I purchased a medium and they fit just above my ankle. Got in both the tan and black! They’re super stretchy and buttery-soft comfy, like a nice pair of leggings. I’ve had them since May. I needed something to wear at my internship in a Physical Therapy clinic, so can be dressed up! Have pockets too! I’ve washed multiple times and I’ve had no pilling. If you decide to dry them, they do shrink a bit but I was wanting a more fit. I gave them 4 stars because of the waistband. I had issues with it rolling down and it was wide that it looked off with a tucked polo in the tan colors. Otherwise BUY NOW!!

Great for big thighs and bums! Like workout leggings after coming out of wash though :( (See update below**) I love these pants! They are surprisingly flattering on my hourglass shape. Right out of the package I thought, "no way" because at first they just feel like work out leggings but they are thicker and more structured than that. They do run large though so size down. I ordered a medium and am ordering a different color in small. For reference I am 5'5" and 170lbs with a medium build and hourglass shape ( 41,31,43) and 36DD and these pants in medium could be tighter for more compression and a better shape. I was a doubter, but no more. Just buy them you'll be happy you did. They seem like they'll wash and dry no problem. If issues with laundering, I will update my review. **UPDATE** these came out of the wash feeling much more like workout leggings and less structured, which is disappointing to me. I have plenty of leggings. I was planning on buying these pants in other colors but have decided to skip them. I ordered the black in a small and those were too small so medium is the sweet spot for me in these pants.

Just what I’ve been looking for. As a curvy person who works in the animal medical industry, I have somewhat struggled to find a good bottom for work. Of course I want to be comfortable, but also require a quality material. I have tried scrubs in the pants, and I’m sure there are good scrub bottoms out there but when you try the Greys Anatomy brand and have them get easily damaged in the thigh area, you give up. (Especially for the price) To make a long story short, these pants seem to be what I’ve searching for. (I haven’t had them for very long but so far so great!) they are very comfortable and feel a bit more professional than pant jogger sweatpants. They’re also a bit fashionable, if you pair them with a nice sneaker. I am 5’5 and a size 16, XL shirt. I don’t agree that they are a bit long, but I typically wear high waisted items and if you wear the pants up high, it’s not an issue. (And keep them tied) After I’ve wear them more and the don’t fail me, I will order more. I’m leaving this review so early to hopefully help out someone that is searching for pants like myself.
